Meanwhile, Tony has to take probationary agent Ned Dorneget under his wing

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: A deep-sea diver is murdered on the job, and his co-workers all become suspects. However, when they return to the surface, they must remain in a high-tech decompression chamber for four days. programme.desc: Siegfried is pleased with his new time-management system - until he misplaces his diary and has an unfortunate encounter with a Great Dane. Rural drama, starring Christopher Timothy and Robert Hardy

programme.title: To the Manor Born
programme.desc: Richard tries to clinch a lucrative cosmetics deal with a glamorous French businesswoman, but when she propositions him, he lies that he has a wife. Now all he has to do is persuade Audrey to pose as Mrs DeVere for the weekend. Comedy, starring Penelope Keith and Peter Bowles, with a guest appearance by Rula Lenska

programme.title: Are You Being Served?
programme.desc: Mr Grace decides to spruce up the ladies' department with a much-needed lick of paint, forcing the female staff to share the men's counter - a sure-fire recipe for disaster. Shop-floor comedy, starring John Inman, Mollie Sugden, Trevor Bannister and Harold Bennett

programme.title: Last of the Summer Wine
programme.desc: Compo shocks everyone by making an effort to dress smartly in an attempt to woo a lady. Peter Sallis and Bill Owen star

programme.title: Last of the Summer Wine
programme.desc: Clegg persuades Compo and Blamire to buy a car so they can get around more easily, but the old banger they end up with turns out to be more trouble than it is worth

programme.title: Judge John Deed
programme.desc: Jo Mills takes on the case of a worker killed on a building site, convinced that the managing director of the company should be held accountable for his neglect of health and safety legislation. However, the Lord Chancellor's department once again try to divert the course of justice to avoid embarrassment - and Deed's ex-wife is appointed counsel for the defence. Legal drama, starring Martin Shaw and Jenny Seagrove

programme.title: New Tricks
programme.desc: The team reinvestigates the death of wealthy financier Douglas Anderson when psychic Sebastian Carter informs the deceased's daughter that she needs to resolve her father's unfinished business. The retired officers set out to prove the clairvoyant is a fraud after growing suspicious of his 'gift', but Pullman struggles to dismiss the supposed conman when a conflict involving her own late father emerges. Detective drama, guest starring David Bradley

programme.title: Waking the Dead
programme.desc: Feature-length episode. A psychiatrist treating a disturbed woman on trial for killing her family asks the team to investigate possible links to a double murder committed three years before, and their inquiries soon turn up a number of similarities. Paul Kaye, Laurence Penry-Jones and Lucy Gaskell guest star, with Trevor Eve and Sue Johnston

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Terry investigates a loan shark accused of using strong-arm tactics to collect the extortionate repayments she demands. Stories about her employing a vicious thug to make acid attacks on the families of debtors prove all too true and Beth finds herself in danger when the ruffian is cornered. Meanwhile, a Ukrainian woman with no passport or visa is taken to hospital following a road accident but runs away before Sally can get the full story

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer

programme.title: Wanted Down Under Revisited
programme.desc: Nicki Chapman catches up with Persephone and Debbie Santa Clara de Magdalene, who sampled life in New Zealand, hoping it might just be the country for them and their children

programme.title: Caught Red Handed
programme.desc: Dominic Littlewood returns with the programmes that reveals how the police and the public are using CCTV evidence and technology to catch criminals in the act. A specialist crime squad stop a mugger on a moped in his tracks, intruders prowl the tiny streets of a model village in the dead of night, and insurance cheats are brought to justice when their fake falls are exposed

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Roo Irvine and Danny Sebastian help two teams look for items in Shrewsbury to sell at an auction in East Yorkshire, while Natasha Raskin Sharp has a mystery for fair-goers to solve

programme.title: Father Brown
programme.desc: The priest walks in on a man standing over the body of his dead wife, while Mrs McCarthy strikes it lucky at the church bingo with the help of a fortune teller her sister has befriended. Guest starring Niamh Cusack

programme.title: The Hairy Bikers' Bakeation
programme.desc: Si King and Dave Myers bike from north to south through Europe, searching for the best baking each country has to offer. They start their 5,000-mile trek in Norway, picking up tips and recipes from professionals and home bakers

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Ginny Buckley helps a couple from Bedfordshire who are looking for a character property in Norfolk with a good-sized garden for some new canine companions, all on their budget of £450,000. Ginny also heads to Great Yarmouth, where she straps in for a white-knuckle ride on one of the country's last remaining wooden rollercoasters

programme.title: The Farmers' Country Showdown
programme.desc: Elwyn and Ann-Sophie take their large range of vegetables to Cowbridge Farmers' Market in South Wales, where pig farmers Liz and Andrew also sell their charcuterie

programme.title: The Bidding Room
programme.desc: Five dealers bid against one another to buy a spice box, two Chinese statues, a funfair horse, a 1900s cabinet and a dummy called Steve. Nigel Havers presents

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Alex Jones presents the first visit of the week to the One Show studio, featuring the usual mix of topical stories and celebrity chat

programme.title: Panorama
programme.desc: In-depth current affairs report covering a story behind the headlines

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Kathy worries when she cannot find Ian and questions Sharon. Lexi is unwell at school, so Ben asks Callum to collect her, only to be told he cannot take her home as he is not family. Alarm bells ring for Linda when she accompanies Frankie to the crematorium on the anniversary of her little brother Harry's death. Chelsea reassures Denise she has not been in touch with Lucas, but tells him he is a changed man

programme.title: Katie Price: Harvey and Me
programme.desc: Model and businesswoman Katie Price's son Harvey was born with a rare disorder that affects brain function, hormones and vision, and he has a long list of medical and behavioural conditions that mean his future is unpredictable. This documentary follows Katie and Harvey as he turns 18, with the pair exploring the options of further education colleges and meeting other families that contain disabled young adults

programme.title: Staged
programme.desc: David and Michael are both upset when they discover who the studio has now cast as they each have a history with the actors taking their place - one of whom is known and the other unknown. Comedy, starring David Tennant and Michael Sheen

programme.title: Staged
programme.desc: Accepting their fate is to no longer be involved in the American project, David and Michael prepare to finally leave their homes. David is finally due to go back to work, Michael thinks he finally has a route to New York, and the girls perform their charity sketch

programme.title: RuPaul's Drag Race UK
programme.desc: The singing, acting and dancing skills of the contestants are tested in Rats - The Rusical, the programme's first- ever live musical. Guest judge and West End star Sheridan Smith joins Michelle Visage and Alan Carr on the judging panel

programme.title: The Graham Norton Show
programme.desc: The host invites award-winning actress Carey Mulligan to talk about her role in the revenge thriller Promising Young Woman. Hollywood and Broadway star Neil Patrick Harris also guests, discussing his appearance in Russell T Davies' 1980s-set serial It's a Sin. Plus, ex-footballer Ian Wright promotes his podcast is Ian Wright's Everyday People, Grantchester star James Norton reflects on his role in the powerful drama Nowhere Special and French actress Camille Cottin also guests. Singer-songwriter Celeste performs her current single Love Is Back

programme.title: The Truth About
programme.desc: Over the past 12 months, a team from Imperial College London, in collaboration with the BBC, have surveyed the mental health of over 350,000 people across the UK during the Covid-19 pandemic. Now, clinical psychologist Professor Tanya Byron joins former England footballer Alex Scott to discover how the latest science can help us gain greater control over our state of mind and improve mental health and wellbeing

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Dan Walker and Louise Minchin present a round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Morning Live
programme.desc: Kym Marsh and Gethin Jones host the weekday magazine show featuring a mixture of topical chat and expert advice

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin Roberts, Dion Dublin and Martel Maxwell visit a seaside property in Blackpool, a three-bedroom semi in Staffordshire and a mid-terrace house in Birmingham

programme.title: Wanted Down Under Revisited
programme.desc: Nicki Chapman catches up with the Langridge family three years on from their trial week in Perth intended to decide if moving to Australia was right for them

programme.title: Caught Red Handed
programme.desc: A woman is dragged to the ground in a doorstep robbery, launderette owners use wanted posters to catch thieves, and criminals drive their own cars into the hands of the police

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: A special edition for BBC Music Day, which sees the Darkness frontman Justin Hawkins and bass guitarist Frankie Poullain competing against Feeder singer Grant Nicholas and bassist Taka Hirose. They'll be scouring an antiques fair in Stamford Meadows, Lincolnshire, with the help of Kate Bliss and Philip Serrell, in a bid to make the most cash - and while they're busy, presenter Natasha Raskin Sharp pays a visit to the BBC's famous Maida Vale studios

programme.title: Father Brown
programme.desc: After Maeve Lochlin's father Pat is accused of murdering Roger Barford, Maeve and her boyfriend Sid Carter travel to Kembleford to ask Father Brown to help them clear his name. However, things don't look good for Pat when it is revealed that he and Roger had an argument about money on the night Roger was killed

programme.title: The Hairy Bikers' Bakeation
programme.desc: The duo bike through the Netherlands, Belgium and Luxembourg, where dishes include Dutch apple pie, Jewish buns, chocolate truffle cheesecake and macaroons with ice-cream

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Steve Brown helps a couple from London find a new home in North Yorkshire, and meets an award-winning cheese maker who has brought a taste of Syria to the British countryside

programme.title: The Farmers' Country Showdown
programme.desc: Donna and her sons go to sell their bacon butties, pulled pork wraps and burgers at Cullompton Food Festival. while Melanie and Peter are looking to sell their unique goat products

programme.title: The Bidding Room
programme.desc: Five dealers bid against one another to buy two mannequins, a Moorcroft vase, an electric massager, a religious piece of art and a curious ladder. With Nigel Havers

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Alex Jones presents another mix of nationwide reports and live studio-based chat

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Chelsea tries to persuade Denise to let Lucas live with her, leading to an ultimatum. A desperate Kathy pushes Sharon for more answers. Linda tries to find the right moment to tell Mick about Jed. A close call for Honey and Jay leads them to a big decision. A jealous Gray watches Kush and Whitney playfully teasing each other, giving him an idea

programme.title: Holby City
programme.desc: Jac must fight for control of the hospital when a returning patient wreaks havoc. Reeling from Skylar's tragedy, the F1s try to cover their tracks when a flirtatious patient makes a serious accusation. Grieving Sacha is scared of letting go of his daughter while a desperate Jodie is on the loose, and his usual arsenal of lies, delays and deceptions may not be enough to keep Beka safe

programme.title: Paramedics on Scene
programme.desc: Documentary following Scottish Ambulance Service crews in Glasgow and Edinburgh as they respond to 999 calls, including one about a child who has fallen in a loch

programme.title: Celebrity Best Home Cook
programme.desc: Claudia Winkleman hosts a star-studded version of the culinary contest, which sees Mary Berry, Chris Bavin and Angela Hartnett judging 10 famous cooks. Taking part are Ferne McCann, Gareth Thomas, Desiree Burch, Ed Balls, Karim Zeroual, Ruth Madeley, Tom Read Wilson, Shobna Gulati, Ed Byrne and Rachel Johnson. In this first edition, Mary wants to know what home cooking means to the contestants, so she challenges them to produce their ultimate home-cooked dinner

programme.title: Match of the Day
programme.desc: Gary Lineker presents highlights of the latest Premier League matches, including West Bromwich Albion v Manchester City. The Baggies surprisingly held City to a 1-1 draw in the reverse fixture, which proved to be Slaven Bilic's last match in charge of the Midlands club. Plus, Newcastle United v Leeds United, Brighton & Hove Albion v Fulham and Southampton v Arsenal

programme.title: Michael McIntyre's The Wheel
programme.desc: Award-winning comedian Michael McIntyre hosts, as contestants take on a colossal spinning wheel and answer questions in the hope of securing the cash prize

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Dan Walker and Louise Minchin present a round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Morning Live
programme.desc: Kym Marsh and Gethin Jones return with the weekday magazine show featuring a mixture of topical chat and expert advice

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: The presenters follow the development progress of properties sold at auction in Blairgowrie in Perth and Kinross, Fenton in Stoke-on-Trent and Chatham in Kent

programme.title: Wanted Down Under Revisited
programme.desc: Catching up with Steve and Diane Hargreaves three years after they appeared on the show to see if they decided to leave the UK behind for a new life in Australia

programme.title: Caught Red Handed
programme.desc: A ruthless gang are caught attacking people who are wearing expensive watches. Plus, a look at some of the terrible disguises criminals have used to try and hide their identities

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Caroline Hawley and David Harper give advice to two teams at Newbury Showground. Plus, host Eric Knowles learns of some fascinating hoards found in the local area

programme.title: Father Brown
programme.desc: When a tortured artist is murdered, Father Brown must discover who committed the crime before it is too late. Starring Mark Williams and Sorcha Cusack

programme.title: The Hairy Bikers' Bakeation
programme.desc: Si King and Dave Myers continue their journey in Germany, proving that German cuisine isn't all sausages and beer, but is based on regional specialities

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Sonali Shah helps buyers who want to relocate from Surrey to north Devon on a £650,000 budget, and visits the annual regatta in the fishing village of Appledore

programme.title: The Farmers' Country Showdown
programme.desc: Beekeepers Debbie and Chris collect honey from their hives, while vegetable and fruit farmers Paul and Jackie gather their crops for the Winchester Farmer's Market

programme.title: The Bidding Room
programme.desc: The dealers bid against each other on items including a unique 1960s vase, a curious mirror, two chalkware statues and a troll called Trotter

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: Party Political Broadcast
programme.desc: By the Liberal Democrats

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Magazine show, with stories of interest from around the UK, plus celebrity guests in the studio. Presented by Alex Jones

programme.title: The Bidding Room
programme.desc: Nigel Havers presents as five dealers bid against one another to buy intriguing items. First to arrive is Princess from Surrey, with an unusual item - a set of wooden, carved apples, which are cut into different numbers of sections and were used to help children learn about fractions. Next, part-time entertainer Chris brings in a bed of nails, before 18-year-old Jude presents the dealers with a beautifully preserved, original account of a First World War Zeppelin raid on Southend

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: Jay Blades and the team of experts bring four treasured family heirlooms back to life. First up, Rose Werner and her sister Linda hope that Suzie Fletcher can turn her expertise to a decorative leather box that they hold dear to their hearts. Peter Coxon is next to arrive, hoping the teamwork of Steve Fletcher and Dominic Chinea can provide a speedy recovery to a favourite childhood toy, a tin speed boat. Later, the experts also turn their attention to an Italian dressing table chair and a tortoise pouffe

programme.title: Celebrity Best Home Cook
programme.desc: The remaining nine talented celebrity home cooks are put through their paces as they face a series of challenges. First, Mary Berry tasks the famous faces to make a chocolate dessert fit for a romantic date night, before the competitors take on Chris Bavin's tricky challenge of rustling up a dish using his favourite cut of leftover meat. The judges must then decide, taking both challenges into account, who is safe and who needs to prove themselves by cooking again in Angela Hartnett's dreaded Eliminator

programme.title: Match of the Day
programme.desc: Gary Lineker presents highlights of the latest Premier League fixtures, including Manchester United v Sheffield United at Old Trafford. Both teams were in need of points for different reasons, with the Red Devils challenging for the title and the Blades struggling at the foot of the table. Plus, Everton v Leicester City, Burnley v Aston Villa, Chelsea v Wolverhampton Wanderers and Crystal Palace v West Ham United

programme.title: Fight for First: Excel Esports
programme.desc: Excel prepare for one of their biggest challenges of the season - a face-off with their UK rivals and former World Champions Fnatic. Not only is this an all-important match, but coach 'Young Buck' has his reputation to play for. He brought Fnatic to glory as their coach in the World Championships, but can Joey beat his old team with the current underdogs of the LEC?

programme.title: A Question of Sport
programme.desc: Former England striker Darren Bent, Great Britain 110m hurdles great Colin Jackson, three-time world gymnastics champion Beth Tweddle and former swimming star Karen Pickering join team captains Matt Dawson and Phil Tufnell and host Sue Barker on the quiz

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Nick Hall and Ochuko Ojiri lead two teams competing at Ardingly Antiques Fair in West Sussex, while presenter Christina Trevanion observes which side raises the greater profit and also looks at the history of English jigsaw company Victory Games

programme.title: The Farmers' Country Showdown
programme.desc: Young farmer Izzi took over from her father aged just 22. She's responsible for a herd of 43 Highland cows and some rare Lincoln Reds. Sisters Laura and Samantha started making award-winning wines on 12 acres of their father's farm four years ago. With lots of overheads and plans for their farms' futures, these women head to the beautiful Creake Abbey Famers' Market to try and fill the coffers. Narrated by Denis Lawson

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce and the team are in Christchurch Park in Ipswich, where items assessed include an heirloom Rolex, memorabilia related to Donald Campbell's land-speed record and a necklace worth half a million pounds. There is also a rare Roman relic dug up in the garden and a pocket watch with a hidden secret

programme.title: The Week in Parliament
programme.desc: A round-up of the week's proceedings in Parliament

programme.title: Politics Live
programme.desc: The latest stories from Westminster and beyond

programme.title: Inside the Human Body
programme.desc: Michael Mosley explores the workings of the human body and tells the stories of people who have pushed their bodies to the absolute limit. He begins at the moment of conception, using state-of-the-art graphics to reveal the sequence of events in the lead-up to a birth. The programme also follows the progress of a couple who are expecting triplets and meets a woman pregnant with her 16th child

programme.title: Put Your Money Where Your Mouth Is
programme.desc: The antiques challenge sees Paul Hayes and John Cameron searching for items at a car boot sale in Essex to sell for profit, with a budget of £250 each

programme.title: Natural World
programme.desc: Documentary telling how the world's smallest animals manage to survive, revealing how they have evolved extraordinary skills and achieved mindboggling feats. From the animal kingdom's greatest artist to the tiny creatures that provide people with so much of the air they breathe, the programme discovers what it takes to be a miniature miracle. Hugh Dennis narrates

programme.title: Meerkat Manor
programme.desc: Tall grasses and beautiful flowers flourish as heavy rains leave the Kalahari covered with lush foliage. Although life is looking good for the Whiskers, the unusual conditions could spell disaster for the family. Narrated by Bill Nighy

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: Paul Martin presents from Capesthorne Hall in Cheshire, where Anita Manning falls in love with a pair of Pilkington vases and Mark Stacey finds an unusual lightbulb. Paul also goes behind the scenes of Capesthorne to find out what it takes to keep a stately home running

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Charlie Brooks, Les Dennis, Darren Harriott and Melinda Messenger go toe-to-toe testing their general knowledge skills in a variety of trivia-based games across the week

programme.title: Lightning
programme.desc: Comedian Zoe Lyons hosts a quick-fire quiz in which six contestants compete across six rounds testing their general knowledge, quick thinking and speedy reactions

programme.title: Lose Weight and Get Fit with Tom Kerridge
programme.desc: The volunteers are short on time for cooking and exercise, so Tom offers solutions to keep them on track, including quick and delicious recipes, and tips for planning ahead. For the first recipe, Tom makes a pot of beef mince - the starting point for some of the nation's favourite dishes. He uses the batch to make a cottage pie, while the rest is frozen to be used later, perhaps for chilli con carne, spaghetti bolognese or lasagne

programme.title: Mastermind
programme.desc: John Humphrys invites four more contestants to brave the black chair, asking them questions on the specialist subjects of Tchaikovsky, the Evil Dead trilogy, Rafael Nadal, and This Country, before they are tested on their general knowledge

programme.title: Only Connect
programme.desc: Victoria Coren Mitchell hosts another second-round match as the Barons and the Colleagues compete for a place in the quarter-finals. The contestants must use patience, lateral thinking and sheer inspiration to make connections between four things that may appear at first not to be linked

programme.title: University Challenge
programme.desc: In the third of the quarter-finals, two teams of four students compete to make it to the next stage of the competition as they try to win two matches and progress to the semi-finals. Jeremy Paxman asks the questions

programme.title: Cornwall: This Fishing Life
programme.desc: The documentary follows the Toms as they try to resurrect the last boat built in Looe and invest in the future of the town's fishing fleet, with three generations throwing themselves into the fray. The Toms' journey to get the boat back out and fishing takes them through the challenges of Britain's quota system, in which just five families own nearly a third of the country's entire fish quota

programme.title: Have I Got a Bit More News for You
programme.desc: Steph McGovern hosts an extended edition of the satirical quiz from October 2019, as comedian and actor Miles Jupp and journalist Camilla Long join regular captains Ian Hislop and Paul Merton to poke fun at the news

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the day's events

programme.title: Confronting Holocaust Denial with David Baddiel
programme.desc: The Holocaust is one of the most documented, witnessed and written about events in history, yet one in six people worldwide either think its scale has been exaggerated or deny that it took place. In this film, comedian David Baddiel explores the multi-faceted nature of Holocaust denial - in both historical and contemporary terms - in an attempt to understand what motivates this phenomenon and why it is on the rise, both in Britain and across the globe

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: Ellie Harrison and Tom Heap head to Somerset to visit the Mendips to find out how this stunning but fragile area is being protected from the invasion of visitors. Ellie scales Cheddar Gorge as well as discovering the damage cars are wreaking on narrow winding roads, and how white-clawed freshwater crayfish are being saved from voracious invaders. Tom finds out how the countryside can help transform lives and also investigates if the government's new green subsidy scheme can really deliver for farmers. Adam Henson meets ex-international rugby union ref turned Nigel Owens, who is now a farmer and facing his herd's first TB test

programme.title: Death in Bollywood
programme.desc: Documentary following the British family of actress Jiah Khan in a search for the truth about her death in Mumbai in 2013, which was ruled a suicide by police. Jiah's mother Rabbiya hired private forensic investigator Jason Payne James, who believes it is highly unlikely that a distinctive oval mark found on the 25-year-old's neck could be caused by the dupatta scarf she was found hanging from in her flat

programme.title: The Moment of Proof
programme.desc: A wedding video cracks the case of a teenager's abduction, and an expired pilot's licence exposes a fraudster flying under the radar. Narrated by Chuku Modu

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.desc: Nicki Chapman catches up with Persephone and Debbie Santa Clara de Magdalene, who sampled life in New Zealand, hoping it might just be the country for them and their children

programme.title: The Farmers' Country Showdown
programme.desc: Elwyn and Ann-Sophie take their large range of vegetables to Cowbridge Farmers' Market in South Wales, where pig farmers Liz and Andrew also sell their charcuterie

programme.title: Gardeners' World
programme.desc: Part two of two. Monty Don and the team look back at the horticultural highlights from 2020 and celebrate the very best inspirational gardens and gardeners up and down the country. There is also another chance to see what viewers were getting up to in their gardens throughout the year

programme.title: Politics Live
programme.desc: The latest stories from Westminster and beyond

programme.title: BBC Bitesize Daily
programme.desc: A look at Science, English and Geography

programme.title: Inside the Human Body
programme.desc: Michael Mosley demonstrates how the human body keeps itself alive, from the first breath to the last. A dramatic water birth is captured in slow-motion and the programme shares the last moments of an 84-year-old as he passes away at home. Plus, people who have adapted to extreme environments, including a world champion free driver and a man who can swim in glacial lakes

programme.title: Put Your Money Where Your Mouth Is
programme.desc: In Sommieres, Paul Hayes caters for the Gallic masses with a 50-piece dinner service, and John Cameron spends big on a huge male torso sculpture

programme.title: Sailing
programme.desc: Action from the Prada Challenger's Trophy, as American Magic, Ineos Team UK and Luna Rossa Challenge competed to be the challengers to Team New Zealand in the 2021 America's Cup

programme.title: Meerkat Manor
programme.desc: Mozart's unborn pups face an uncertain fate, and the mum-to-be assesses her options as she tries to avoid losing her second litter of the year. Meanwhile, Flower's newest arrivals are welcomed into the family

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: At Muncaster Castle in Cumbria, experts Thomas Plant and Catherine Southon choose items for auction, including a collection of First World War memorabilia and an array of postcards

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Charlie Brooks, Les Dennis, Darren Harriott and Melinda Messenger go toe-to-toe testing their general knowledge skills in a variety of trivia-based games across the week

programme.title: Lightning
programme.desc: Comedian Zoe Lyons hosts a quick-fire quiz in which six contestants compete across six rounds testing their general knowledge, quick thinking and speedy reactions

programme.title: Back in Time for...
programme.desc: The Arderns adjust to 1970s life, a time when supermarkets had become Britain's shopping destination of choice and corner shops were beginning to feel the pinch. The family's experience begins with a grand opening designed to compete against the onslaught of the supermarkets. While Sam dons his chicken costume to drum up trade for the shop's 'cheap cheap' prices, the Lord Mayor of Sheffield cuts the ribbon to open the doors. But as a newly self-service operation, Dave must completely transform his approach to keep the tills ringing

programme.title: Winterwatch
programme.desc: Chris Packham is live in the New Forest, looking for Dartford warblers, and Iolo Williams is looking at the water shrews and discovering their amazing ability to survive the winter. Plus, Megan McCubbin presents the first of a four-part look at species that aren't as loved as they should be, starting with rats.

programme.title: This World
programme.desc: Part one of two. Investigation into the initial Covid-19 outbreak in Wuhan, examining the gulf between what the authorities knew and what was made available to the public. Over the course of 54 days, Chinese officials maintained that the outbreak was under control, with only a few cases linked to a live animal market. However, the infection soon proved to be far more widespread, necessitating stricter lockdown measures

programme.title: Elis James: Funny Nation
programme.desc: The stand-up explores the history of Welsh comedy, beginning by looking at the role of stereotypes in comic portrayals in the company of Rob Brydon, Ruth Jones and Rhod Gilbert. Elis also meets some of the new generation of performers who are breaking through, and hears about their attitudes towards Welsh cliches in comedy

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the day's events, with Emily Maitlis

programme.title: 1944: Should We Bomb Auschwitz?
programme.desc: In 1944 two Jewish prisoners escaped from Auschwitz. After they told the world of the horror of the Holocaust, it raised one of the greatest moral questions of the 20th century - should the Allies bomb the camp? This documentary tells the whole of the story, combining dramatic reconstructions of the arguments that took place at the highest levels with first-hand testimony from survivors to explore a moral dilemma that still resonates today - how to act in the face of genocide

programme.title: Death in Bollywood
programme.desc: Jiah Khan' family believe that was murdered, and her actor boyfriend Sooraj Pancholi is their prime suspect. In this instalment of the documentary, Pancholi tells his side of the story, with the actor facing charges of abetment of suicide as police believed he had driven her to take her own life

programme.title: Death in Bollywood
programme.desc: After two new enquiries conclude that Jiah Khan had died by suicide, the actress's family commission a legal case review from a British law firm, which finds there were faults with the police investigations from the night she died onwards and that vital evidence may have been missed

programme.title: The Moment of Proof
programme.desc: A van's everyday sat nav puts a pair of prolific animal thieves behind bars. Elsewhere, an anonymous letter holds the key to cracking the identity of a blackmailer

programme.title: See Hear
programme.desc: Young deaf activists Hamza, James, and Abigail learn what needs to be done to improve the future of their community. With voiceover

programme.title: Nigella's Cook, Eat, Repeat
programme.desc: Nigella Lawson shares recipes featuring one of her favourite ingredients - the anchovy. She prepares a creamy dressing for the fish, served with bitter leaves and walnuts, and then creates an elixir, combining silvery salted anchovies with garlic and oil. This not only makes an excellent dip for red chicory, but also the perfect accompaniment to a platter of sliced seared steak. Nigella creates an anchovy pasta dish of spaghetti with chard and chilli, and also makes a lemon blossom cocktail and a lemon and elderflower pudding

programme.title: Politics Live
programme.desc: The latest stories from Westminster and beyond

programme.title: BBC Bitesize Daily
programme.desc: Maths, Science and T&D and Wellbeing Wednesday with Katie today is all about resilience

programme.title: Inside the Human Body
programme.desc: Michael Mosley traces human development from birth to adulthood, exploring how a newborn makes sense of the world and revealing how many brain connections are lost between the ages of 11 and 20 as an essential part of growing up. Plus, Moken sea gypsy children who train themselves to see clearly underwater and a Vietnamese girl who speaks 11 languages

programme.title: Put Your Money Where Your Mouth Is
programme.desc: Paul Hayes and John Cameron visit an auction in Warrington and go head to head over a cameo brooch, and Paul resorts to sneaky tactics to hide items from his rival

programme.title: Hidden Kingdoms
programme.desc: Documentary revealing the survival techniques used by the animal kingdom's smallest creatures. North America's Sonoran Desert is home to the grasshopper mouse, which is capable of disarming scorpions and centipedes twice its size, and displays a power and athleticism that enables it to avoid the attentions of rattlesnakes and tarantulas. In Africa, the elephant shrew, known as the sengi, employs speed to keep its distance from predators, following a meticulously constructed - and maintained - network of tiny racetracks in the savannah. The film concludes with Hidden Kingdoms Revealed, a 10-minute report on how the documentary was made. Narrated by Stephen Fry

programme.title: Meerkat Manor
programme.desc: Winter returns and an abandoned Mozart tries to look after her newborn pups alone. Meanwhile, the rival Commandos force a confrontation with the Whiskers. Narrated by Bill Nighy

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: Interesting finds from the show's travels on visits to Muncaster Castle in Cumbria, the Bowes Museum in Co Durham, the Grand Pier in Weston-super-Mare in Somerset and Norwich Cathedral. Items range from an art deco pair of Lalique vases to a 9.5mm projector. Presenter Paul Martin also visits Powis Castle in mid-Wales and finds out about their collection of Indian artefacts

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Charlie Brooks, Les Dennis, Darren Harriott and Melinda Messenger go toe-to-toe testing their general knowledge skills in a variety of trivia-based games across the week

programme.title: Lightning
programme.desc: Comedian Zoe Lyons hosts a quick-fire quiz in which six contestants compete across six rounds testing their general knowledge, quick thinking and speedy reactions

programme.title: Back in Time for...
programme.desc: The Arden family experience corner shop life in the 1980s and 1990s, when stores changed from primary shopping destinations to a place for last minute impulse buys and were forced to make the maximum use of their floor space to stock thousands of products. The programme examines the impact of new legislation allowing supermarkets to open on Sundays, previously the only advantage small businesses had over them. ABC's Martin Fry visits the shop and reveals how the band used to use empty industrial units as cheap rehearsal space, and there is a look at the rise of the gaming industry and a revival of a famous 1980s marketing stunt

programme.title: Winterwatch
programme.desc: Chris Packham is live in the New Forest, looking at what's been happening in the badger sett and at the latest action from the River Ness. Elsewhere, Gillian Burke is at RSPB Old Moor, where she explores the life-cycle of the eel, and Megan McCubbin goes swimming with jellyfish in a bid to understand why they are misunderstood

programme.title: DeLorean: Back from the Future
programme.desc: Documentary charting the rise and fall of John Z DeLorean and his doomed attempt to build the sports car of the future in 1980s war-torn Belfast. The programme features rare and unseen footage filmed by Oscar winning directors DA Pennebaker and Chris Hegedus, offering the first in-depth psychological profile of DeLorean

programme.title: Tomorrow's Worlds: The Unearthly History of Science Fiction
programme.desc: Dominic Sandbrook concludes his exploration of the sci-fi genre by considering the allure of time travel, with the power to change the past or see the future being a theme that writers and film-makers have embraced for many years. From HG Wells' pioneering scientist in The Time Machine, to Doc and Marty McFly in Back to the Future, audiences have been presented with a host of colourful travellers and their machines. Featuring contributions by former Doctor Who stars David Tennant and Karen Gillan as well as current showrunner Steven Moffat, actor Christopher Lloyd and screenwriter Bob Gale (Back to the Future), Quantum Leap star Scott Bakula and The Time Traveler's Wife author Audrey Niffenegger

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the day's events, presented by Emily Maitlis

programme.title: The Last Survivors
programme.desc: Documentary compiling the personal testimonies of the remaining Holocaust survivors living in Britain, all of whom were children during their incarceration in the concentration camps. They discuss how these experiences continue to influence their adult lives and why particular memories have stayed with them. The film follows one man on a return journey to Auschwitz, accompanied by his daughter, while another survivor revisits his home town in Germany for the first time since 1946

programme.title: A Perfect Planet
programme.desc: Examining the solar force that powers Earth and how plants and animals have adapted to the uneven amounts of sunlight given to different parts of the planet over the course of a year. The exception is on the equator, where the duration of day and night remain the same throughout the year. Here, this guaranteed sunlight drives the great diversity of life in the tropical jungles

programme.title: The Moment of Proof
programme.desc: How a satnav revealed the mastermind behind the biggest armed robbery ever to hit Devon and Cornwall, and a hoodie helped police take a dangerous attacker off the streets

programme.title: See Hear
programme.desc: Young deaf activists Hamza, James, and Abigail learn what needs to be done to improve the future of their community. With voiceover

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: The Diary of Anne Frank
programme.desc: The families get used to their incarceration and adapt to the strict rules of daily quiet by which they must live. Anne finds Mrs van Daan increasingly hard to bear and another Jewish man, Mr Dussel, joins them in hiding and becomes the butt of all the teenager's jokes and frustrations

programme.title: The Joy of Painting: Winter Specials
programme.desc: Bob Ross paints a scene depicting a beautiful snowy day outside through a windowpane

programme.title: Secrets of the Museum
programme.desc: Victoria & Albert curators Steph and Jenny plan a major new exhibition celebrating the work of British fashion designer Mary Quant. A Yorkshire woman donates a 1960s minidress that would be perfect - but it needs a lot of restoration work to make it exhibition-ready. Curator Julius uncovers some of the first objects ever given to the museum, including a hand-painted writing case that once belonged to Queen Victoria, while Corinna hits the streets to gather the most significant designs of today - the flags and logo of climate change activists Extinction Rebellion

programme.title: Mark Kermode's Secrets of Cinema
programme.desc: The critic examines the qualities necessary for a film to become a cult classic, as well as the main types of a genre that is dictated by the audience rather than the film-maker. Marks reveals how some set out to shock and break taboos, while others are camp classics embraced by viewers who find new or hidden meanings in them

programme.title: Score: Cinema's Greatest Soundtracks
programme.desc: Hollywood composers including Hans Zimmer, James Cameron, Danny Elfman, John Williams, Quincy Jones and Trent Reznor combine to give a privileged look inside the musical challenges and creative secrecy of the film score

programme.title: Australian Hits at the BBC
programme.desc: A look at a wide-ranging selection of songs and music acts that came from Down Under, with clips of their appearances on some top UK shows. Featured chartbusters include pop pioneers the Easybeats, rock 'n' roll legends AC/DC, and INXS who became one of the nation's most successful acts of all time. Also included are ground-breaking Aborigine band Yothu Yindi, and the more familiar faces of Nick Cave, John Farnham, Kylie Minogue and Jason Donovan

programme.title: Top of the Pops
programme.desc: A compilation of classic Kylie Minogue performances from the Top of the Pops studio

programme.title: The Joy of Painting: Winter Specials
programme.desc: Bob Ross paints a scene depicting a beautiful snowy day outside through a windowpane

programme.title: Mark Kermode's Secrets of Cinema

programme.title: The Diary of Anne Frank
programme.desc: November 1942 arrives and Otto fears he and his family will be discovered after learning the building in which they are hiding is due to be sold. Anne fights with Mr Dussel over the use of the desk in their bedroom and the teenager proves her mettle by holding Mrs van Daan down while Miep extracts one of her teeth

programme.title: The Joy of Painting: Winter Specials
programme.desc: Bob Ross invites you to anticipate winter's white blanket as he creates a soft, delicate cabin landscape draped in snow

programme.title: Inside Obama's White House
programme.desc: Exploring how Barack Obama got re-elected for a second term and tried to tackle America's most intractable social problems - guns, immigration and race. It begins with President Obama's decision to launch the special operation into Pakistan, to catch Osama Bin Laden. As his re-election campaign gears up, Obama has to make a tough choice on contraception - whether to side with the Catholic bishops, or Health Secretary Kathleen Sebelius and feminist activists. Obama chooses the women and they contribute to his convincing election victory

programme.title: Life of a Mountain: A Year on Helvellyn
programme.desc: Documentary capturing a year in the life of the Lake District's most popular peak, featuring insights from those that live by, care for and visit the mountain

programme.title: Mountain
programme.desc: Documentary exploring how the human desire to climb mountains has grown over the past 300 years, with the world's highest points becoming objects of fascination when they would once have been shunned. Featuring footage from the world's highest peaks shot by leading high altitude cinematographers and original music performed by the Australian Chamber Orchestra. Narrated by Willem Dafoe

programme.title: Spiral
programme.desc: Laure confronts Gilou after he is instrumental in helping Cisco's gang acquire a stash of weapons for the upcoming heist. Meanwhile, Edelman must learn how to best act in Lola's defence. In French

programme.title: Spiral
programme.desc: Laure tells Ali that Gilou has infiltrated Cisco's gang and they'll need to play for time in their investigation to protect him - but their efforts are jeopardised when Judge Bourdeiou orders the arrest of Cisco's son Titi

programme.title: The Joy of Painting: Winter Specials
programme.desc: The artist demonstrates how to paint a cabin in a winter landscape, complete with soft misty background and rich trees and bushes

programme.title: Queen Victoria's Children
programme.desc: The concluding episode focuses on the monarch's sons, examining how they struggled to assert themselves in the shadow of their father. It explores Victoria's difficult relationships with the eldest, Bertie, whose sexual indiscretions she blamed for having fatally weakened her husband's health, and Leopold, who put up the most determined effort to break free from his mother's control

programme.title: The Windermere Children
programme.desc: In August 1945, psychologist Oscar Friedmann and his team of counsellors look after child survivors of the Holocaust who were sent to the Lake District to begin new lives. Friedmann and art therapist Marie Paneth, philanthropist Leonard Montefiore and sports coach Jock Lawrence have four months to help the children reclaim their lives, while waiting for news of their loved ones. Fact-based drama, starring Thomas Kretschmann, Romola Garai, Tim McInnerny and Iain Glen

programme.title: The Windermere Children: In Their Own Words
programme.desc: The story of the pioneering project to rehabilitate child survivors of the Holocaust on the shores of Lake Windermere, with first-hand testimonies from some of those who attended. Reflecting on the survivors' lives after Windermere, the film includes home-movie footage and footage of their annual reunions

First up, Rose Werner and her sister Linda hope that Suzie Fletcher can turn her expertise to a decorative leather box that they hold dear to their hearts. Peter Coxon is next to arrive, hoping the teamwork of Steve Fletcher and Dominic Chinea can provide a speedy recovery to a favourite childhood toy, a tin speed boat. Later, the experts also turn their attention to an Italian dressing table chair and a tortoise pouffe

programme.title: Celebrity Best Home Cook
programme.desc: The remaining nine talented celebrity home cooks are put through their paces as they face a series of challenges. First, Mary Berry tasks the famous faces to make a chocolate dessert fit for a romantic date night, before the competitors take on Chris Bavin's tricky challenge of rustling up a dish using his favourite cut of leftover meat. The judges must then decide, taking both challenges into account, who is safe and who needs to prove themselves by cooking again in Angela Hartnett's dreaded Eliminator

programme.title: Match of the Day
programme.desc: Gary Lineker presents highlights of the latest Premier League fixtures, including Manchester United v Sheffield United at Old Trafford. Both teams were in need of points for different reasons, with the Red Devils challenging for the title and the Blades struggling at the foot of the table. Plus, Everton v Leicester City, Burnley v Aston Villa, Chelsea v Wolverhampton Wanderers and Crystal Palace v West Ham United

programme.title: Fight for First: Excel Esports
programme.desc: Excel prepare for one of their biggest challenges of the season - a face-off with their UK rivals and former World Champions Fnatic. Not only is this an all-important match, but coach 'Young Buck' has his reputation to play for. He brought Fnatic to glory as their coach in the World Championships, but can Joey beat his old team with the current underdogs of the LEC?

programme.title: A Question of Sport
programme.desc: Former England striker Darren Bent, Great Britain 110m hurdles great Colin Jackson, three-time world gymnastics champion Beth Tweddle and former swimming star Karen Pickering join team captains Matt Dawson and Phil Tufnell and host Sue Barker on the quiz

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Dan Walker and Louise Minchin present a round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Morning Live
programme.desc: Kym Marsh and Gethin Jones return with the weekday magazine show featuring a mixture of topical chat and expert advice

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Nick Hall and Ochuko Ojiri lead two teams competing at Ardingly Antiques Fair in West Sussex, while presenter Christina Trevanion observes which side raises the greater profit and also looks at the history of English jigsaw company Victory Games

programme.title: The Hairy Bikers' Bakeation
programme.desc: Si King and Dave Myers bike from north to south through Europe, searching for the best baking each country has to offer. They start their 5,000-mile trek in Norway, picking up tips and recipes from professionals and home bakers

programme.title: Wanted Down Under Revisited
programme.desc: Nicki Chapman catches up with Persephone and Debbie Santa Clara de Magdalene, who sampled life in New Zealand, hoping it might just be the country for them and their children

programme.desc: All the latest developments as Scotland responds to the coronavirus pandemic, plus answers to the many questions you have during these unprecedented times

programme.title: Father Brown
programme.desc: The priest walks in on a man standing over the body of his dead wife, while Mrs McCarthy strikes it lucky at the church bingo with the help of a fortune teller her sister has befriended. Guest starring Niamh Cusack

programme.title: Caught Red Handed
programme.desc: Dominic Littlewood returns with the programmes that reveals how the police and the public are using CCTV evidence and technology to catch criminals in the act. A specialist crime squad stop a mugger on a moped in his tracks, intruders prowl the tiny streets of a model village in the dead of night, and insurance cheats are brought to justice when their fake falls are exposed

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Ginny Buckley helps a couple from Bedfordshire who are looking for a character property in Norfolk with a good-sized garden for some new canine companions, all on their budget of £450,000. Ginny also heads to Great Yarmouth, where she straps in for a white-knuckle ride on one of the country's last remaining wooden rollercoasters

programme.title: The Farmers' Country Showdown
programme.desc: Elwyn and Ann-Sophie take their large range of vegetables to Cowbridge Farmers' Market in South Wales, where pig farmers Liz and Andrew also sell their charcuterie

programme.desc: Five dealers bid against one another to buy a spice box, two Chinese statues, a funfair horse, a 1900s cabinet and a dummy called Steve. Nigel Havers presents

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Alex Jones presents the first visit of the week to the One Show studio, featuring the usual mix of topical stories and celebrity chat

programme.title: Panorama
programme.desc: In-depth current affairs report covering a story behind the headlines

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Kathy worries when she cannot find Ian and questions Sharon. Lexi is unwell at school, so Ben asks Callum to collect her, only to be told he cannot take her home as he is not family. Alarm bells ring for Linda when she accompanies Frankie to the crematorium on the anniversary of her little brother Harry's death. Chelsea reassures Denise she has not been in touch with Lucas, but tells him he is a changed man

programme.title: Katie Price: Harvey and Me
programme.desc: Model and businesswoman Katie Price's son Harvey was born with a rare disorder that affects brain function, hormones and vision, and he has a long list of medical and behavioural conditions that mean his future is unpredictable. This documentary follows Katie and Harvey as he turns 18, with the pair exploring the options of further education colleges and meeting other families that contain disabled young adults

programme.title: Staged
programme.desc: David and Michael are both upset when they discover who the studio has now cast as they each have a history with the actors taking their place - one of whom is known and the other unknown. Comedy, starring David Tennant and Michael Sheen

programme.title: Staged
programme.desc: Accepting their fate is to no longer be involved in the American project, David and Michael prepare to finally leave their homes. David is finally due to go back to work, Michael thinks he finally has a route to New York, and the girls perform their charity sketch

programme.title: RuPaul's Drag Race UK
programme.desc: The singing, acting and dancing skills of the contestants are tested in Rats - The Rusical, the programme's first- ever live musical. Guest judge and West End star Sheridan Smith joins Michelle Visage and Alan Carr on the judging panel

programme.title: The Graham Norton Show
programme.desc: The host invites award-winning actress Carey Mulligan to talk about her role in the revenge thriller Promising Young Woman. Hollywood and Broadway star Neil Patrick Harris also guests, discussing his appearance in Russell T Davies' 1980s-set serial It's a Sin. Plus, ex-footballer Ian Wright promotes his podcast is Ian Wright's Everyday People, Grantchester star James Norton reflects on his role in the powerful drama Nowhere Special and French actress Camille Cottin also guests. Singer-songwriter Celeste performs her current single Love Is Back

programme.title: The Truth About
programme.desc: Over the past 12 months, a team from Imperial College London, in collaboration with the BBC, have surveyed the mental health of over 350,000 people across the UK during the Covid-19 pandemic. Now, clinical psychologist Professor Tanya Byron joins former England footballer Alex Scott to discover how the latest science can help us gain greater control over our state of mind and improve mental health and wellbeing

programme.title: Father Brown

programme.title: The Hairy Bikers' Bakeation
programme.desc: Si King and Dave Myers continue their journey in Germany, proving that German cuisine isn't all sausages and beer, but is based on regional specialities

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Sonali Shah helps buyers who want to relocate from Surrey to north Devon on a £650,000 budget, and visits the annual regatta in the fishing village of Appledore

programme.title: The Farmers' Country Showdown
programme.desc: Beekeepers Debbie and Chris collect honey from their hives, while vegetable and fruit farmers Paul and Jackie gather their crops for the Winchester Farmer's Market

programme.title: The Bidding Room
programme.desc: The dealers bid against each other on items including a unique 1960s vase, a curious mirror, two chalkware statues and a troll called Trotter

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Magazine show, with stories of interest from around the UK, plus celebrity guests in the studio. Presented by Alex Jones

programme.title: The Bidding Room
programme.desc: Nigel Havers presents as five dealers bid against one another to buy intriguing items. First to arrive is Princess from Surrey, with an unusual item - a set of wooden, carved apples, which are cut into different numbers of sections and were used to help children learn about fractions. Next, part-time entertainer Chris brings in a bed of nails, before 18-year-old Jude presents the dealers with a beautifully preserved, original account of a First World War Zeppelin raid on Southend

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: Jay Blades and the team of experts bring four treasured family heirlooms back to life. First up, Rose Werner and her sister Linda hope that Suzie Fletcher can turn her expertise to a decorative leather box that they hold dear to their hearts. Peter Coxon is next to arrive, hoping the teamwork of Steve Fletcher and Dominic Chinea can provide a speedy recovery to a favourite childhood toy, a tin speed boat. Later, the experts also turn their attention to an Italian dressing table chair and a tortoise pouffe

programme.title: Celebrity Best Home Cook
programme.desc: The remaining nine talented celebrity home cooks are put through their paces as they face a series of challenges. First, Mary Berry tasks the famous faces to make a chocolate dessert fit for a romantic date night, before the competitors take on Chris Bavin's tricky challenge of rustling up a dish using his favourite cut of leftover meat. The judges must then decide, taking both challenges into account, who is safe and who needs to prove themselves by cooking again in Angela Hartnett's dreaded Eliminator

programme.title: The Top Table
programme.desc: Young people come to the Top Table to face down our politicians in an exciting audience debate show, presented by Stephen Nolan

programme.title: Match of the Day
programme.desc: Gary Lineker presents highlights of the latest Premier League fixtures, including Manchester United v Sheffield United at Old Trafford. Both teams were in need of points for different reasons, with the Red Devils challenging for the title and the Blades struggling at the foot of the table. Plus, Everton v Leicester City, Burnley v Aston Villa, Chelsea v Wolverhampton Wanderers and Crystal Palace v West Ham United

programme.title: Fight for First: Excel Esports
programme.desc: Excel prepare for one of their biggest challenges of the season - a face-off with their UK rivals and former World Champions Fnatic. Not only is this an all-important match, but coach 'Young Buck' has his reputation to play for. He brought Fnatic to glory as their coach in the World Championships, but can Joey beat his old team with the current underdogs of the LEC?

programme.title: A Question of Sport
programme.desc: Former England striker Darren Bent, Great Britain 110m hurdles great Colin Jackson, three-time world gymnastics champion Beth Tweddle and former swimming star Karen Pickering join team captains Matt Dawson and Phil Tufnell and host Sue Barker on the quiz

programme.title: House of Lords
programme.desc: Debate in the Lords on a UK government statement on UK fishing rights post-Brexit, from Tuesday 19 January

programme.title: Ten Minute Rule Bill
programme.desc: Freedom of Speech at Universities Bill. The introduction of a bill on freedom of speech at universities, from Tuesday 19 January

programme.title: Select Committees
programme.desc: Commons Public Accounts Committee session on the UK border post-Brexit, with evidence from HMRC, the UK Department of Transport, and UK Border Force, from Thursday 21 January

programme.desc: It was once the richest democracy in Latin America, but under President Nicolas Maduro, Venezuela has been in an economic tailspin. programme.desc: Ben Shephard hosts the quiz in which contestants answer questions to win turns on an arcade-style machine. Dropping tokens down a choice of four chutes, they hope to knock piles of them off a moving shelf - and the more they collect, the greater the prize fund. The player who has won the least amount is then eliminated, and the last one standing competes for a £10,000 jackpot

programme.title: The Chase
programme.desc: Bradley Walsh presents as contestants Emma, Jamie, Mandy and Paul pit their wits against quiz genius the Chaser. Answering general knowledge questions, they each add as much money as they dare to the jackpot - but if anyone is caught they're out for good, leaving fewer players for the final chase

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: Debbie has a proposal. Dawn has devastating news. Moira tries to get through to Mackenzie

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Debbie distances herself from Ray's crimes. Aggie fears that Michael is falling for Grace again. Ken despairs at Peter's drunkenness

programme.title: Bradley Walsh & Son: Breaking Dad
programme.desc: The actor and his son continue their European adventure in Switzerland, where they take a ride on a mountain rollercoaster before exercising their vocal chords when they try the Swiss tradition of yodelling. The following day, Barney arranges a unique way for them to enjoy some local cheese, and then drive to the Contra Dam, where Barney reveals they are about to attempt one of James Bond's most famous stunts

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: The residents learn their houses are safe from redevelopment. Adam keeps a close eye on Peter. Michael lays down the law to Aggie

programme.title: Long Lost Family
programme.desc: Davina McCall and Nicky Campbell present two stories of people searching for missing relatives who were found much closer than they could ever have imagined. Pauline Pedder was looking for the daughter who she put up for adoption 50 years ago, while Donna Cowell wanted to find out what happened to her younger brother

programme.title: ITV News at Ten
programme.desc: A fresh perspective on the major stories of the day, featuring national and international news, with reports and analysis from ITV's correspondents

programme.title: On Assignment
programme.desc: Rageh Omaar presents reports from around the world. Rohit Kachroo is in Puerto Rico to discover what people feel about the possibility of their country becoming the 51st state of America, Juliet Bremner explores how extreme surfing has transformed the Portuguese seaside town of Nazare, and Debi Edward attends an ice and snow sculpture festival in China

programme.title: Paul Sinha's TV Showdown
programme.desc: Chaser, stand-up comedian and TV trivia genius Paul Sinha hosts this TV-themed comedy quiz show. programme.title: Tipping Point
programme.desc: Ben Shephard hosts the quiz in which contestants answer questions to win turns on an arcade-style machine. The player who has won the least amount is then eliminated, and the last one standing competes for a £10,000 jackpot

programme.title: The Chase
programme.desc: Bradley Walsh presents as contestants Ellen, Kevin, Josh and Neha, who have never met before, pit their wits against one of the ruthless Chasers in the hope of winning a potential prize pot worth thousands of pounds. They work as a team and play strategically to answer general knowledge questions against the clock and race down the game board to the exit without being caught

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: Priya is relieved, Harriet makes a big decision and Al is broken

programme.title: South Africa with Gregg Wallace
programme.desc: The MasterChef presenter takes a 300-kilometre road trip along the stunning coast of the Western Cape, heading for Gansbaai, otherwise known as Shark Town. He stops for lunch at a South African fish and chip shop, releases rehabilitated African Penguins back into the wild, and braves a boat trip through shark-infested waters

programme.title: Martin Clunes: Islands of Australia
programme.desc: The actor begins the next part of his journey on Mundoo Island, which has provided shelter and a way of life for five generations of one farming family. Martin Clunes joins Colin and Sally Grundy for a day of cattle mustering. He also explores Phillip Island, one of Australia's favourite playgrounds, then heads south to King Island, in the blustery Bass Strait between Tasmania and mainland Australia. Martin completes his odyssey with a visit to see one of the region's most iconic creatures, the Tasmanian Devil

programme.title: Marcella
programme.desc: Return of the crime drama, starring Anna Friel. Marcella is now based in Belfast, working as an undercover detective. She has taken on a new identity, Keira, and has infiltrated the infamous Maguire crime family. But as she investigates their activities, questions come to the fore about how much she's embraced the Keira personality and left Marcella behind. Amanda Burton and Hugo Speer join the cast for the third series

programme.title: Marcella
programme.desc: Rav heads to Belfast to investigate Bobby while Marcella delves deeper into the Maguires' criminal world. Crime drama, starring Anna Friel

programme.title: ITV News at Ten
programme.desc: A fresh perspective on the major stories of the day, featuring national and international news, with reports and analysis from ITV's correspondents

programme.title: The Bay
programme.desc: After dealing with the repercussions of her actions from last year, DC Lisa Armstrong is given the opportunity to step up when asked to assist a murder investigation in Morecambe. Tensions remain high as Lisa struggles to do her job whilst following the orders of her previously lower ranking colleague, Med. As the team tries to uncover why the victim was targeted, Lisa and her children are unknowingly stalked by a mysterious figure. Return of the crime drama, starring Morven Christie, Daniel Ryan and Stephen Tompkinson

programme.desc: A short bulletin rounding up the day's stories

programme.title: Love Your Garden
programme.desc: Alan Titchmarsh and his team surprise a foster parent in Nottingham, creating a lush Caribbean haven that evokes her father's garden in Jamaica. Mother-of-two Fiona Kirlew has fostered more than 40 children, despite only being 36 years old, and was nominated by one of her former foster children as a gesture of just how much they owe to Fiona's commitment to giving them the best start in life

programme.title: The Cabins
programme.desc: Away from the trappings of modern-day dating, three more 24-hour dates get underway. Nature has brought an end to this year's mating season, but for one lucky dater there's a second chance of love just before the Cabins closes its doors. In the cabin next door, they have a big decision to make before they leave, but nothing a bit of axe throwing won't help them figure out - will the final couple be a match made in heaven?

programme.title: Love Bites
