programme.title: A Place in the Sun: Home or Away
programme.desc: Jasmine Harmin and Laura Hamilton go head to head to help Sue and David Sherlock from Stockport. The couple want a fresh start with their daughter, but cannot decide where to start their new adventure. David is championing the Lake District, close to his three grown-up daughters, but Sue wants a complete change and thinks their future lies in the Dordogne region of France. Jasmine is given a UK budget of £730,000, but it's going to be close as Laura tries to entice them across the Channel with three attractive properties in France worth up to £500,000

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: Simon O'Brien and Henry Cole head to Surrey and Hampshire. Simon builds a huge kitchen table from discarded rafters and gives a 1970s campervan a new lease of life. Henry hankers after an American tractor and Royal Enfield motorbike and sidecar

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: The programme heads to Lowestoft in Suffolk, where Henry Cole picks a valuable Lyons Tea enamel sign while Simon O'Brien converts a station trolley into a garden sofa

programme.title: Escape to the Chateau: DIY
programme.desc: DIY fanatic Fiona contends with sub-zero temperatures as she sets out to renovate a chateau bedroom for her daughter, who is due to visit in two months, while new owners Erin and JB aim to transform the first floor of their property's south wing

programme.title: Building the Dream
programme.desc: Charlie Luxton meets Chris and Sonya Bennett, who are planning to build a modern, environmentally friendly family home in Hampshire. The couple knock down a bungalow to put up a two-storey building, with Chris at the helm of the project - but they run into difficulties after a smooth start

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The competition begins at the White Lodge Hotel in Filey, North Yorkshire, where hosts James and Kim Hodgson hope their love of high standards will win over their guests

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Amanda Lucia-Charley and her friend Alan Talbot invite the other hoteliers to sample the delights of Crystals on the Prom in Blackpool, where Alan introduces his alter-ego Ada

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The third visit is to the Old Red Lion Country Inn in Upper Poppleton, North Yorkshire, where pair Mark and Jean Lee hope their plain and simple pub B&B will impress their guests

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The contest ends at glamping site Camp Katur in Kirklington, North Yorkshire, where Kerry Roy and Nicola Wilding hope to wow their guests with their unique 'geodomes' and see-through pods

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The final day of the contest arrives, and while Amanda Lucia-Charley and Alan Talbot receive harsh criticism of their breakfast, Mark and Jean Lee's decor receives scathing reviews

programme.title: George Clarke's Amazing Spaces
programme.desc: The architect rediscovers some of the best tiny builds to be found up in the treetops across the world. In Tuscany, George visits a romantic treehouse, while in Sweden there's a tree hotel shaped like a UFO. The programme also features a tree house designed like a mirrored cube as well as a look back at the epic treehouse George and master carpenter Will Hardie built together in Kielder Forest Park

programme.title: The Secret Life of the Zoo
programme.desc: An elephant calf is born at Chester Zoo, but its young half-sister doesn't welcome the new arrival. Meanwhile, bush dog dad Franco is under pressure as its family grows, and an onager - a rare type of wild horse - faces a major operation

programme.title: Escape to the Chateau: DIY
programme.desc: in and JB turn their attention to transforming their chateau's ruined orangery into a top-class events venue. When the concrete is delivered for a new floor, JB must race against time to find a missing piece of kit before the concrete sets. Tensions bubble to the surface as a new spa is delivered to Tim and Sasha's chateau, while Fiona celebrates her birthday and continues to battle sub-zero temperatures as she renovates a bedroom for her daughter Rochelle

programme.title: Grand Designs
programme.desc: Leigh Osborne and Graham Voce want to convert and extend a landmark 150-year-old water tower in Kennington, south London, into a luxury home. Grade II-listed, derelict and with 6ft-thick walls, it's a huge challenge for the pair, who also plan to build two structures at the base - a lift shaft connected by a series of glass tunnels, and a modern living space. The result will be a four-bedroom property over nine floors, complete with a room at the top offering spectacular 360-degree views across the capital. Presented by Kevin McCloud

programme.title: Auschwitz Untold: In Colour
programme.desc: A powerful and revelatory account of one of the most hideous crimes in human history told from the perspectives of 16 extraordinary Holocaust survivors. The survivors retell of the rise of fascism, the uprooting of families, and the long journey from centuries-old Jewish communities into Auschwitz. The film includes a rare and haunting collection of photographs called the 'Auschwitz Album', and chronicles with unflinching detail the survival challenges at Auschwitz-Birkenau, as well as tales of resistance inside the camp, including sabotaging armaments for the Nazi war machine

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: Consultant Emer Sutherland helps treat an eight-year-old who has fallen 8ft onto concrete while riding his BMX, losing his front teeth and possibly injuring his head and spine. A 19-year-old motorcyclist who hit a bollard requires urgent treatment on his leg, which may otherwise need to be amputated, and X-rays of a stabbing victim yield startling results

programme.title: 8 Out of 10 Cats Does Countdown
programme.desc: Sean Lock and Katherine Ryan take on Jon Richardson and Joe Wilkinson in the words-and-numbers quiz, while Adam Riches as Sean Bean joins Susie Dent in Dictionary Corner. Hosted by Jimmy Carr, with regular Countdown mathematician Rachel Riley looking after the numbers and letters

programme.title: Auschwitz Untold: In Colour
programme.desc: A powerful and revelatory account of one of the most hideous crimes in human history told from the perspectives of 16 extraordinary Holocaust survivors. The survivors retell of the rise of fascism, the uprooting of families, and the long journey from centuries-old Jewish communities into Auschwitz. The film includes a rare and haunting collection of photographs called the 'Auschwitz Album', and chronicles with unflinching detail the survival challenges at Auschwitz-Birkenau, as well as tales of resistance inside the camp, including sabotaging armaments for the Nazi war machine

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: Consultant Emer Sutherland helps treat an eight-year-old who has fallen 8ft onto concrete while riding his BMX, losing his front teeth and possibly injuring his head and spine. A 19-year-old motorcyclist who hit a bollard requires urgent treatment on his leg, which may otherwise need to be amputated, and X-rays of a stabbing victim yield startling results

programme.title: Food Unwrapped
programme.desc: Kate Quilton learns that a special enzyme contained in pineapples attacks and destroys dead skin cells in the mouth, with scientists using this enzyme to treat the victims of burns. Jimmy Doherty wants to uncover the secret of how bubbles are added to chocolate bars and Matt Tebbutt asks if there is any truth in the old wives' tale that we should never eat mussels when there's not an ‘R' in the month

programme.title: Impossible Engineering
programme.desc: The creation of London's Crossrail network, a project of staggering proportions, with 10,000 personnel working across 40 sites and on 118km of track. The focus should have been on the impressive engineering feat, though delays and budgets have become the focus of attention

programme.title: Impossible Engineering

programme.desc: Jamie Theakston investigates the truth behind the legend of King Arthur, and looks for evidence of whether the mythical monarch was English, Scottish or Welsh

programme.title: Narrow Escapes of World War Two
programme.desc: The story of the siege of Kohima in 1944, when heavily outnumbered British and Indian troops held off Japanese forces until reinforcements could arrive

programme.title: World War Weird
programme.desc: Bizarre events during wartime, featuring a Nazi anti-gravity device, phantom cities built during the Second World War, Stalin's cursed tomb and poultry of mass destruction

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo has a go at playing Winchester Cathedral's Victorian organ as he continues his tour of Britain's railways. He also goes behind the scenes at a rail works in Eastleigh that began operating in the 19th century, and travels to the Isle of Wight, one of Queen Victoria's favourite holiday destinations

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo journeys from Brockenhurst to Poole. He heads to the New Forest, where he seeks venomous adders, discovers a library in Wimborne containing rare books and visits 19th-century potteries that are still trading today

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce heads to Scotland, where a crowd awaits the opinions of the experts at the National Gallery of Modern Art in Edinburgh. Objects include a small carved ivory figure from the 18th century ploughed up in a local field, an original manuscript by poet Robert Burns and a small box which, having been purchased recently for just £30, delivers an early Christmas present for its owner

programme.title: Steam Train Britain
programme.desc: Behind the scenes of three preserved steam railway lines - the South Devon Railway heritage line, the Ffestiniog and Welsh Highlands Railway and the Great Central Railway. Sports scientist Megan Smith has a dream of tending the fire of a steam locomotive on the Ffestiniog & Welsh Highland Railway, and she stokes the bolier of NG143 and sets off along the 25 miles from Caernarfon to Porthmadog

programme.title: Bangers and Cash
programme.desc: Behind the scenes of a North Yorkshire-based, family-run historic car auction business, which uncovers some remarkably rare vehicles in the picturesque village of Thornton-le-Dale

programme.title: Abandoned Engineering
programme.desc: An abandoned prison beneath the water, a tunnel in the heart of a mountain, concrete towers hidden in the woods and a rusting coastal relic

programme.title: World War Weird
programme.desc: A Japanese secret society's repeated attempts to kill Charlie Chaplin and Hitler's plan in 1942 to use educated dogs to do a soldier's job

programme.title: Narrow Escapes of World War Two
programme.desc: The story of Major General Roy Urquhart, who successfully evaded the enemy in his retreat across the Rhine after leading British troops into a doomed attack at Arnhem

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce heads to Scotland, where a crowd awaits the opinions of the experts at the National Gallery of Modern Art in Edinburgh. Objects include a small carved ivory figure from the 18th century ploughed up in a local field, an original manuscript by poet Robert Burns and a small box which, having been purchased recently for just £30, delivers an early Christmas present for its owner

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo continues following the route Queen Victoria took from Windsor Castle to the Isle of Wight, and reveals the oil fields hidden beneath some of England's seaside resorts. During his journey, he also explores the role the Dorset town of Weymouth played in the D-Day landings during the Second World War, and visits the site of Victorian Britain's most famous building rock in Portland

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo sets off on a journey from Oxford, through the Malvern Hills toward south Wales. He gets a special view of the city of Oxford, and discovers a unique and colourful crop in the Cotswolds

programme.title: Chris Tarrant: Extreme Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Chris Tarrant returns for more train journeys through some of the world's most challenging terrain. He heads to the Arctic for a trip along the Alaska Railroad, but as he arrives in the port of Seward, it's not a great start as there are no trains running, so he ends up hitching a ride with a track inspector's car. He continues his journey on a freight train heading for Anchorage, Alaska's biggest city, and finally heads north on the Aurora passenger train to the town of Healy. Along the way he checks out the tiny village of Eklutna, which has great importance for the indigenous inhabitants, and goes for an intrepid fishing trip on a thawing frozen lake

programme.title: David Jason: Planes, Trains and Automobiles
programme.desc: The actor discovers the machines that made America. Here, he samples the luxury of 1930s aviation, drives the car that changed the world and climbs aboard a vintage steam train

programme.title: Steam Train Britain

During his journey, he also explores the role the Dorset town of Weymouth played in the D-Day landings during the Second World War, and visits the site of Victorian Britain's most famous building rock in Portland

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo sets off on a journey from Oxford, through the Malvern Hills toward south Wales. He gets a special view of the city of Oxford, and discovers a unique and colourful crop in the Cotswolds

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce looks back at the most talked-about finds of the past 12 months and updates their stories to find out what happened to the items once the cameras stopped rolling. They include the story of a travelling chest that once belonged to a Queen of England - and how it ended up at Hampton Court Palace - the vicar who thought he found a lost masterpiece by the Flemish painter Van Dyck in an antiques shop, and an object valued at £5,000 12 years ago which delivered the largest sale price of any item seen on the show

programme.title: Steam Train Britain
programme.desc: At the Ffestiniog and Welsh Highlands Railway, manager Stephen Greig is trying to ensure that all goes to plan ahead of the opening of the new station at Caernarfon

programme.title: Bangers and Cash
programme.desc: A rare 1980s Sierra Cosworth draws in a crowd of admiring Ford fans, while a restorer delights buyers with his immaculate 1966 Sunbeam Rapier and Sarah takes a shine to a caravan

programme.title: Abandoned Engineering
programme.desc: Concrete channels hidden in a mountaintop forest, a collapsing bridge, a marooned military base and the upright rusting carcass of ship

programme.title: World War Weird
programme.desc: The unfortunate fate of the world's first tank, which failed to make it onto the battlefield, and the case of the Japanese soldier who continued fighting - until 1974

programme.title: Narrow Escapes of World War Two
programme.desc: The story of Lieutenant General Leslie Morshead, who commanded the British and Australian troops who held off Rommel's Afrika Korps at the port of Tobruk in 1941

programme.title: James May's Cars of the People
programme.desc: The presenter examines how the car industry in Germany and Japan blossomed following the Second World War, becoming manufacturing giants that far outstripped the Allied nations that had defeated them. He test drives some of the vehicles Germany and Japan produced during the post-war period, as well as an assortment of other classic cars, including E-Types and Mustangs. He also reveals the sad tale of the Austin Allegro, a vehicle that had a devastating impact on the British car industry

programme.title: One Foot in the Grave
programme.desc: Victor Meldrew finds himself forced into early retirement, but is determined not to end up on the scrapheap, pouring scorn on the modern world and driving everybody crazy in the process. Richard Wilson stars in the first-ever episode as misery guts Meldrew, with Annette Crosbie as his long-suffering wife. From 1995

programme.title: One Foot in the Grave

programme.desc: Ninety-three-year-old D-Day veteran Thomas Hassell brings along a treasured item with a proud history - a smart military swagger stick awarded to him while on army service in Berlin for consistently being the smartest soldier on parade. The stick is a cherished symbol of a long and proud career, but sadly the decades have not been kind, and the silver head is badly dented and discoloured. programme.title: How It's Made

programme.title: A New Life in the Sun

programme.title: A Place in the Sun

programme.title: A Place in the Sun: Winter Sun
programme.desc: Dee and Maurice Evans have decided the time is right to buy a holiday home close to the theme parks in South Orlando. Laura Hamilton guides them through the booming Florida market, showing them five properties for their £200,000 budget

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: The first host in Bournemouth, 38-year-old bar manager and musician Adie, hopes to hit the right notes by ensuring his night of laid back fun is full of music and laughter. With trumpets and drums at the ready and tricky crustaceans proving hard to crack, does Adie have what it takes to win the £1,000 prize?

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: The second Bournemouth host is aristocratic vet Sir Henry, who plans to charm his way to the prize by hosting an evening of relaxed dining

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Countryside-loving Pippa from Bournemouth hopes to impress her guests by throwing a dinner party with every course inspired by fish

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: In Bournemouth, spiritual student Simone hopes to wow her guests with a spicy Bajan menu. She's keen to impress her new friend, posh vet Sir Henry, but she's unsure about Pippa, who doesn't hold back as she probes Simone about her student house

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: On the last night in Bournemouth, flamboyant charity manager Jacqueline hopes that Michelin star-style food will win her the prize. With partridge and black pudding scotch eggs, this could be a culinary night to remember, but will Sir Henry and Simone steal her thunder with an unlikely romance?

programme.title: The King of Queens
programme.desc: Carrie becomes close friends with the new neighbour, leaving a jealous Doug feeling unwanted. American comedy set in New York, starring Kevin James

programme.title: The King of Queens
programme.desc: Doug tries to get out of going to the bank with Carrie, claiming he is too busy at work, but a stapler-inflicted injury in a delicate area makes him see the error of his ways. Comedy, starring Kevin James

programme.title: Ramsay's Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: In the first of a two-part edition, Gordon Ramsay heads to La Galleria 33 in Boston, Massachusetts, an Italian restaurant run by sisters Rita and Lisa. The pair grew up working at their father's eatery nearby, but when they opened their own business, they couldn't replicate his success, and they now blame their failure on everything from the weather to the tablecloths

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Essex couple Belinda and Alan have decided to take the leap and buy a property in or around the pretty seaside resort of La Cala de Mijas in Spain's Costa del Sol. They call upon the expertise of Laura Hamilton, who finds five options to show them within their £160,000 budget

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Property expert Scarlette Douglas offers advice to scuba-diving couple Caitlin and Simon, who want to buy a dream holiday home on the idyllic Greek island of Paros, famous for its gorgeous beaches and traditional villages. Scarlette hopes to make a splash with the five options she lines up for their £140,000 budget

programme.title: Junior Bake Off

programme.title: George Clarke's Amazing Spaces

programme.title: Maze Runner: The Death Cure (2018)
programme.desc: A group of young people try to save their friends who are being held captive. Final part of the sci-fi saga based on James Dashner's novels, starring Dylan O'Brien, Ki Hong Lee and Kaya Scodelario

programme.title: 999: What's Your Emergency?
programme.desc: Documentary series following the work of emergency services. The entire process is captured, from the moment a call is taken at the control centres to the deployment of the police, ambulance teams and fire crews, following them to the front line as they deal with the unpredictable, the shocking and the sometimes disturbing

programme.title: 999: What's Your Emergency?
programme.desc: This edition focuses on how South Yorkshire police tackle the issues of sexual offences, with officers called to a suspected sexual assault outside a nightclub, while in another early-morning incident, officers confront a man after he exposes himself in the street. Call handlers deal with a case of sexual assault on a teen by other teenagers, and a man is arrested on suspicion of domestic rape as a result of a call from his wife

programme.title: Back
programme.desc: Return of the comedy series starring David Mitchell and Robert Webb. After a long absence, a rejuvenated Stephen heads back to the John Barleycorn, just as Andrew is starting to feel restricted and weighed down by the mundanities of being a provincial pub landlord. However, the opening of a rival pub just up the road evokes curiosity - especially when it becomes apparent that the new establishment needs a manager

programme.title: It's a Sin
programme.desc: Russell T Davies' drama, set in the 1980s during the Aids crisis, following the lives of a group of young gay men. September 1981 finds 18-year-old Ritchie leaving the Isle of Wight to go to university. In London, Roscoe walks out of parents' home, while Colin arrives from Wales to start a new career on Savile Row. The three men meet, and along with fellow student Ash and best friend Jill form a gang and move into a flat, christened the Pink Palace. However, a distant threat on the horizon means that life in the 1980s might be all too precious. Olly Alexander, Omari Douglas and Callum Scott Howells star

programme.title: 8 Out of 10 Cats Does Countdown
programme.desc: Katherine Ryan and Joe Lycett take on Johnny Vegas and Sean Lock in the words-and-numbers game, while would-be novelist Christopher Bliss joins Susie Dent in Dictionary Corner. Hosted by Jimmy Carr with Rachel Riley looking after the numbers and letters

programme.title: Coach Trip

programme.title: A New Life in the Sun
programme.desc: Two ex-casino employees start a new life in Portugal's Algarve running a restaurant, while a horse lover from Somerset prepares to move to Italy to open a pony-trekking business

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Fred and Susan live and work on a farm in Norfolk and are looking for somewhere they can escape to during the quieter winter months. They would like a two-bed property in or near Palma, Majorca's sought-after capital, so Jonnie Irwin lines up five options for their budget of £265,000

programme.title: A Place in the Sun: Winter Sun
programme.desc: With retirement on the horizon, Eve and Vinden Grace are looking for a place that they can use for holidays for now, but where they will eventually live half the year. The Gulf Coast of Florida has the perfect blend of lifestyle, culture and climate. Laura Hamilton shows them five homes for their £100,000 budget in and around Clearwater

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: In Swansea, performing arts director Anthony plans a theatre-themed menu for his guests, Ross, Tula, Kola and Julie. However, straight-talking Tula tries to steal the show with a candid revelation and Julie brings the house down with some frisky fun at Kola's expense. Anthony's plans go awry in the kitchen, threatening to earn him more rotten earn tomatoes than rave reviews

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Salesman Ross hosts the second party from Swansea, but his guests make a discovery that leaves him with some explaining to do, while tension erupts between him and Kola

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Social carer Kola provides plenty of hip-hop swagger on the third day of the contest in Swansea, with a Nigerian menu he hopes will both educate and impress his fellow diners

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Triathlete Tula serves a Spanish-themed menu in Swansea, hoping charm and chorizo can smooth over the dispute between Ross and Julie and avoid a punch-up over the paella

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Social worker Julie plans a chilled out slumber party for the final night of the week in Swansea. programme.title: The King of Queens
programme.desc: Deacon and Kelly ask Doug and Carrie to be godparents to their newborn son. Comedy, starring Kevin James, Leah Remini and Victor Williams

programme.title: Ramsay's Kitchen Nightmares USA

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Jasmine Harman goes house hunting with Mandy and Paul from Sussex around the picturesque Spanish town of Dolores. They're dreaming of a permanent move to their favourite part of the Costa Blanca with their six cats and rescue dog and would love to find a house with plenty of outside space for their furry family. Jasmine manages to find five properties within their maximum budget of £185,000, but whether any of them will be liked by her clients remains to be seen

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Danni Menzies helps John, a widower from County Durham, search for a new property in rural, inland Malaga in Spain, with a budget of £140,000

programme.title: Junior Bake Off
programme.desc: Harry Hill introduces two international challenges on the second day of finals week - a Japanese inspired technical and tartlets combining flavours from around the world

programme.title: Jamie and Jimmy's Friday Night Feast
programme.desc: In an episode filmed in 2019, Jamie Oliver and Jimmy Doherty are joined by guest Joe Lycett. Jamie teaches Joe to make a traditional wild boar and pappardelle ragu from the restaurant he spent time in while holidaying in Italy, and the comedian wears an authentic Italian housecoat to complete the look. Jamie also celebrates the weekend with a scrumptious veggie tikka masala, with beautiful marinated and grilled veggies served with paneer cheese and amazing little pancakes to mop up the classic sauce. Jimmy has a smoking hot tip for oven-cooked barbecue ribs that looks impressive, but is actually super simple to do

programme.title: Grand Designs
programme.desc: Kevin McCloud follows the progress of Nathan and Amye as they build a giant cathedral-like property modelled on Dutch barn style houses, but with a contemporary twist. It is a hugely ambitious plan, and with Nathan project managing and setting themselves just a year to finish the build, the pressure to pull it off is huge

programme.title: Kirstie and Phil's Love It or List It
programme.desc: Kirstie Allsopp and Phil Spencer meet Gary and Bill in the Edinburgh suburb of Portobello, who are unsure whether to sell their 19th-century four-bed home or renovate instead. Bill loves their home's character, quirkiness and close proximity to the seaside, but Gary hates the busy road and the home's layout, and would like to settle down somewhere new

programme.title: Escape to the Chateau: Make Do and Mend
programme.desc: Dick and Angel Strawbridge help Natalie and Pete come up with some compact camping ideas, such as a portable solar shower that doubles up as a sink and tyre chairs with a rustic rope finish. programme.title: A Place in the Sun: Winter Sun

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: It is the second night from Essex/Suffolk, and it is the turn of salon owner Sam to cook. She has opted for a good looking, simple menu, but foodie Chris is not impressed

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Day four in Essex sees Polish party girl Joanna offer a taste of her homeland in a bid to win the prize. But some of the unusual flavour combinations throw her guests

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: On the final night of the competition in Essex and Suffolk, ex-policeman Mark wants to surprise his guests with some homemade favourites. Meanwhile, will there be wedding bells or tears when Chris answers Joanna's earlier proposition?

programme.title: The King of Queens
programme.desc: Doug hurts Arthur's feelings, so he takes him to a baseball game to apologise. However, his efforts to catch a foul ball lead to a pitch invasion and a brush with the law

programme.title: The King of Queens
programme.desc: Doug and Carrie throw a dinner party with Ray and Debra Barone - but just when things seem to be going well, the guys get into trouble. Ray Romano and Patricia Heaton guest star, with Kevin James and Leah Remini

programme.title: Ramsay's Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: The chef travels to Newbury Park in southern California to help Mexican restaurant Mama Rita's. The owner previously ran a successful catering company but has struggled to adapt her skills to meet the demands of a new business - and with a year's worth of food stored in the freezer Gordon realises he has a tough job ahead

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Musician Donagh is taking the giant step of relocating to the vibrant Spanish city of Valencia. With best friend Aime coming along to help him look for a new forever home, he calls on the house-hunting skills of Scarlette Douglas to find him the perfect city pad. Donagh has a maximum budget of £125,000 and a long wishlist, so the pressure is on Scarlette to deliver

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Nurse Karen and GP Alastair from Montrose long to relocate to the beautiful countryside of the Dordogne in France. Laura Hamilton shows them five options for their £175,000 budget

programme.title: First Dates
programme.desc: Two divorcees go in search of a more successful match, and a woman who despairs of ever finding the right man finds her head turned from her date to another man in the restaurant. A barbershop owner tries to settle his nerves by drinking heavily before the date, and an unconventional neuroscientist looks for love with an artistic woman who has always put other people's needs first, but now wants something for herself

programme.title: 8 Out of 10 Cats Does Countdown
programme.desc: Sean Lock and Paul Foot play take on Jon Richardson and Angela Barnes in the words-and-numbers game, while poet John Cooper Clarke joins Susie Dent in Dictionary Corner. Hosted by Jimmy Carr with Rachel Riley looking after the numbers and letters

programme.title: Grand Designs
programme.desc: Kevin McCloud meets energy conservationist Andrew, who has decided to build a radical, self-heating home for his family - the first of its kind in the UK. He aims to heat the house all year round by storing the warmth of the summer sun into insulated earth banks over and around the house, and then drawing the warmth back into the living spaces in winter. However, this pioneering construction seems an exercise in trial and error from the start, and what began as a seemingly simple, pure concept becomes a complicated feat of engineering

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E

programme.title: Secret Safari: Into the Wild
programme.desc: Documentary exploring Kenya's 900,000 wildlife conservancy Ol Pejeta, providing a safe home to a vast range of species including elephants, rhinos, hippos and lions to hyenas, ostriches and cocktail ants. A hungry pride of lions needs to make a kill, but their best hunter has gone missing. An endangered rhino gives birth, but the rangers fear the calf will be vulnerable to predators

programme.title: World's Strongest Man 2018
programme.desc: James Richardson presents action from Europe's Strongest Man in Leeds, where Hafthor Julius Bjornsson - also known as 'The Mountain' in Game of Thrones - entered as the defending champion, but faced stiff competition from the likes of Konstantine Janashia and Mateusz Kieliszkowski. With commentary by Colin Bryce and Danny Wallace

programme.title: World's Strongest Man 2018
programme.desc: James Richardson presents action from the North American Open in Martinsville, Indiana, as the Giants Live Tour continued. Rob Kearney. Two British competitors in Laurence Shahlaei and Mark Felix, and former WWE wrestler Evan Singleton were among those looking to take Kearney's title from him. With commentary by Colin Bryce and Danny Wallace

programme.title: World's Strongest Man 2018
programme.desc: James Richardson presents action from the World Tour Finals at the Manchester Arena in Manchester, as the Giants Live Tour concluded. Four-time World's Strongest Man Zydrunas Savickas made a surprise return in this event last year and claimed the title. With commentary by Colin Bryce and Danny Wallace

programme.title: The Nightmare Neighbour Next Door
programme.desc: Wiltshire couple Edward Lea and his wife found their happy retirement was short-lived when an imposing 8ft breeze-block wall - dubbed the Great Wall of China - was built by their neighbour between their properties. In Scotland, Karen Ryrie and her elderly mother are fed up with living opposite John Tyer, his three dogs and a huge family of rabbits. Following their complaint about the smell, Karen's subsequent accusation of assault has made the dispute far more serious. Elsewhere, Linda Bird had not been expecting to wear headphones around the house when she moved to Bedfordshire for a quiet life, but the issue has escalated beyond excessive noise to become a battle over boundaries with neighbour Stanislav Fajdel

programme.title: The Nightmare Neighbour Next Door
programme.desc: A dispute between households arising from investments made into a business venture that later failed, and the fury surrounding the construction of an extension that saw damage to an adjacent property. Plus, updates on people and stories featured in previous programmes, including the latest whereabouts of rowdy rapper Elvis Turasinze, who drove his neighbours berserk with his beat-making

programme.title: Can't Pay? We'll Take It Away!
programme.desc: Experienced enforcement agent Stewart and trainee Iain head to Blackpool to chase down a builder owing nearly £3,500 to an unhappy customer, while father and son Del and Dael are in Essex to collect over £2,500 owed to a frozen food company. In south London, Del and Brian attempt to recover nearly £10,000 from a debtor who acted as guarantor for a friend's rental agreement, ending in a bizarre stand-off with the debtor's daughter

programme.title: Can't Pay? We'll Take It Away!
programme.desc: High Court Enforcement agents evict a large group of squatters barricaded into a disused nursery in London, and have to resort to smashing their way in as they refuse to leave. Meanwhile, agents in Bristol try to recover over £10,000 owed by a plumbing business to a supplier, and emotions run high when they attempt to evict a tenant who pleads her innocence in west London

programme.title: Can't Pay? We'll Take It Away!
programme.desc: An episode combining new and unforgettable past cases. The High Court Enforcement Agents head to Manchester to track down over £5,000-worth of debt, and seize an expensive car in the process. Later in south London, the agents deal with a violent debtor who threatens them with a concealed weapon, and in Liverpool, they are met with lies and aggression from a mechanic who owes nearly £3,000

programme.title: Can't Pay? We'll Take It Away!
programme.desc: Stewart and Vic become party to a fractured family's school fee woes in Staffordshire and Del and Dael need to reclaim £3,500 after a rental van was returned badly damaged. Stewart and Elmor are in Warwickshire, where the members of a household blame their predicament on a scheming landlord whose subsequent arrival sparks a fierce reaction, while Iain helps Stewart remove a young mother from her Lancashire home

programme.title: Neighbours
programme.desc: Kirsha arrives for Christmas, determined to bring her parents back together, but Dipi is not the only one warming up to Shane. Chloe reveals the truth despite Amy's objections

programme.title: Home and Away
programme.desc: Jasmine saves Colby from prison time by putting up her assets to afford bail. Once out, Colby gets to work to find out who ratted him out. Mac confronts her brother

programme.title: Criminals: Caught on Camera
programme.desc: Nick Wallis examines how CCTV and helicopter cameras mean wrongdoers can no longer hide in a crowd. He highlights the use of such footage to help seal the conviction of a man involved in the August 2011 riots across England, and reports on a state-of-the-art network monitoring a football crowd of 21,000 as Leicester City take on Barnsley

programme.title: Fare Dodgers: At War with the Law
programme.desc: A prolific offender is landed with an eyewatering bill to square off three years of illegal travel, while the police must step in when one suspect is found in possession of a banned substance. At one of London's busiest stations, vigilant inspectors Scott and Michelle have success intercepting repeat offenders, but will they catch a notorious passenger who the station staff report comes through their station each day?

programme.title: Bargain Brits on Benefits
programme.desc: Cameras follow folks whose clever ideas and savvy schemes save them a small fortune

programme.title: And Proud
programme.desc: Documentary following a group of new arrivals from their Roma village to London, originally shown to coincide with EU work restrictions being lifted. Members of Roma families already established in the UK also talk about their experiences

programme.title: The Gypsies Next Door
programme.desc: In the Irish town of Thurles, the council want Barry to move into new housing purpose-built for travellers. But they will not allow him to take his 30 horses. Teenager Scarlett, who lives with her traveller family in Luton, dreams of being a successful model - and non-traveller model Danielle Mason is going to help show her the way

programme.title: Ambulance: Code Red
programme.desc: Documentary following emergency response teams on their most extreme medical callouts

programme.title: A&E After Dark
programme.desc: A man who fell through a window has a phobia of needles, which leads him to refuse any kind of pain relief and even attempt to discharge himself before his injuries are treated. An aggressive patient is brought in by four police officers after smashing up his mother's home, and has to undergo a toxicology test. Dr Audrey Fox tries to save the life of a patient who has overdosed, and a woman suffers a head injury after slipping on a slice of pizza her husband had left on the floor for their dog to eat

programme.title: Rich Kids Go Homeless
programme.desc: Six young privileged Brits, who believe that homelessness is often the result of the victim's own actions, agree to live on the streets of London for three nights. They include 23-year-old Kieren, who plans to beg to raise money that he will invest in umbrellas. On his first night he only makes £5, and finds a place under the arches near Trafalgar Square to bed down. Woken up by security, Kieren begs for several more hours until he makes enough to stay in a hostel

programme.title: World's Strongest Man 2018
programme.desc: James Richardson, Eddie Hall and Michelle Ackerley present action from heat two of the showpiece strongman event, held in Manila, the Philippines. America's Brian Shaw began his bid for a record-equalling fifth title, with Great Britain's Adam Bishop among the men standing in his way. With commentary by Colin Bryce and Danny Wallace

programme.title: World's Strongest Man 2018
programme.desc: James Richardson, Eddie Hall and Michelle Ackerley present action from heat three of the showpiece strongman event, held in Manila, the Philippines. One of the most decorated strongmen of all time Zydrunas Savickas was involved in this heat and looking to prove he could still compete with the best at the age of 42. With commentary by Colin Bryce and Danny Wallace

programme.title: The Nightmare Neighbour Next Door
programme.desc: The story of a dispute involving a female student and the man living upstairs, which descended into violence. Plus, how Doug and Mavis moved into a new house with their menagerie of animals and were stunned when a neighbour complained about the noise. But that turned out to be just the beginning of their troubles

programme.title: The Nightmare Neighbour Next Door
programme.desc: Carol and Trevor Gilmour hoped to renovate a barn and turn it into their dream home, but have been forced to live in a caravan during a long-running dispute with their neighbour over the property's front garden. Plus, a young couple gather shocking footage of the constant harassment they endured, and a farmer is suspected of killing a family's dogs

programme.title: Can't Pay? We'll Take It Away!
programme.desc: An episode combining new and unforgettable past cases. The High Court Enforcement Agents head to Manchester to track down over £5,000-worth of debt, and seize an expensive car in the process. Later in south London, the agents deal with a violent debtor who threatens them with a concealed weapon, and in Liverpool, they are met with lies and aggression from a mechanic who owes nearly £3,000

programme.title: Can't Pay? We'll Take It Away!
programme.desc: The agents chase almost £3,000 owed by Indian takeaway owners, but when the staff deny any knowledge of the debt, the agents threaten to seize kitchen equipment. In east London they face a race against time to recover more than £8,000 from an electrical services company, and in Cheshire the enforcement agents chase nearly £4,000 of debt owed by a mobile phone shop

programme.title: Can't Pay? We'll Take It Away!
programme.desc: The enforcement officers visit a former professional footballer who owes £4,500 in unpaid legal fees, and in London the officers chase up a £4,000 debt owed by a car dealer. Agents Gary Brown and Cona Jackson are involved in a serious altercation with a young man who owes almost £11,000, who threatens the officers with a knife when they start listing goods to seize to offset the debt

programme.title: Can't Pay? We'll Take It Away!
programme.desc: The officers chase a debt of almost £2,000 in unpaid parking fines, and later encounter the extraordinary case of a debt of £80,000 owed by a hotel owner to his ex-girlfriend. However, as they begin to search the property, they call upon police back-up as the agents fear the debtor may have access to firearms. Elsewhere, two officers head to a car garage in Manchester where the owner owes £2,000 after losing a court case over a faulty vehicle

programme.title: Neighbours
programme.desc: Shane accidentally makes Dipi think he wants another shot at their marriage. Hendrix considers a morally questionable plan to pay off his debt

programme.title: Home and Away
programme.desc: Tori has to set up Grace's birthday in time, rescuing Tori from a cake disaster in the process. Nikau worries for his uncles, and Mac cannot keep her concerns hidden anymore

programme.title: Benefits Britain: Life on the Dole
programme.desc: Documentary chronicling the ups and downs of life for people on benefits in the UK, beginning with a visit to Liverpool to look at the effect of the bedroom tax. Lorna was living in a three-bedroom housing association home with her children, but when they moved out she was hit with a weekly £40 bedroom tax, which she has to pay out of her £72.40 employment and support allowance. Keith and Marie had been in their family home for 35 years, but after refusing to pay an extra £24 they were rehoused in a one-bedroom flat and forbidden from taking any of their animals with them

programme.title: Criminals: Caught on Camera
programme.desc: Journalist Nick Wallis joins police forces out on the road and in the air to discover how CCTV and technological advances are being used to fight crime. Footage includes some residents taking neighbourhood watch to the next level, a group of thugs caught wrongdoing on a housing estate and a pensioner using her walking stick to defend herself against an intruder

programme.title: Countdown to Murder
programme.desc: Documentary examining some of Britain's most notorious murders, charting the last few days leading up to the killing from the perspectives of both perpetrator and victim. The programme uses dramatic reconstruction to pinpoint the trigger that set each one in motion, providing in-depth psychological analysis and scrutiny of the devious and calculating plans the killers formulated

programme.title: Born to Kill?
programme.desc: A psychological profile of former nurse Beverly Allitt, who is currently serving 13 life sentences for murdering four children and injuring nine others at Grantham Hospital in Lincolnshire in 1991. Psychologists and criminology experts explore the possible motives for her crimes, examining her behaviour during childhood, when she was known to be an attention seeker

programme.title: Harry Brown (2009)
programme.desc: An elderly retired marine leads a law-abiding existence on a south London estate, until his best friend is murdered by a local gang. Furious at the police's inability to bring the killers to justice, he takes the law into his own hands to get revenge. Crime thriller, starring Michael Caine, Emily Mortimer, Charlie Creed-Miles, Ben Drew, Iain Glen and Sean Harris

programme.title: The Nightmare Neighbour Next Door

programme.title: Law & Order
programme.desc: A woman is murdered during an apparent road-rage incident, and the officers unravel a conspiracy involving arson. American crime drama starring Sam Waterston

programme.title: Law & Order
programme.desc: Lupo and Bernard try to solve the murder of a 17-year-old youth, and discover his younger brother had recently been banished from a polygamous Mormon sect. As they investigate the group's controversial leader, the detectives learn he had been trying to track down one of his wives. Crime drama, starring Jeremy Sisto, Anthony Anderson and Sam Waterston, with Colm Meaney (Star Trek: The Next Generation)

programme.title: Law & Order
programme.desc: Lupo and Bernard investigate when a man is crushed to death under a crane. Initially they suspect the construction company of foul play, but the case leads them to the victim's hospitalised wife, as well as plans for a controversial medical procedure

programme.title: Law & Order
programme.desc: Part two of three. A witness reveals that personal trainer Newman flew to Los Angeles on the night of the murder, leading the officers to believe they have the wrong man. When blood matching the dead film studio executive's is found in their new suspect's car, they soon arrest him but their case may not be clear-cut enough to warrant a conviction. Sam Waterston stars

programme.title: Law & Order
programme.desc: An investigation is launched when a war widow with a possible grudge against the army is found unconscious in the rubble of a bombed recruitment centre alongside her lifeless child. Lupo and Bernard begin their search for the bomber and encounter a number of suspects, including a man whose sister was killed in Iraq and the leader of a group of war protesters

programme.title: Law & Order
programme.desc: The murder of a religious webster creator leads Bernard and Lupo to a network of corruption in a charitable foundation that provides the motive for the crime. The case becomes even more complicated when the suspect manages to avoid prosecution by being granted asylum, leaving McCoy fighting for his career

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: A petty officer is killed while competing in a swimsuit contest, raising fears she may have fallen victim to a jealous rival, or even a stalker. Gibbs' investigation leads to the discovery that the victim was taking yoga classes - and this provides him with the breakthrough he needs to solve the case. Crime drama, starring Mark Harmon and Pauley Perrette

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: An officer is found dead, and evidence initially suggests she committed suicide as a result of a nervous breakdown. However, a sceptical Ducky believes foul play was involved - and the team later discovers the victim was caught up in a love triangle. Kevin Quigley guest stars

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: A US Marine is murdered, but it is not long before the prime suspect is also found dead. Tim and Abby discover a clue to both killings in an e-mail that suggests the victims were involved in a game with deadly consequences. Drama, starring Sean Murray and Pauley Perrette

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: A US Marine suspected of murdering a girl is killed in Iraq, and a search of his personal belongings reveals her body. Most members of the team believe they have found conclusive evidence of his guilt - but Gibbs suspects there is more to the situation than meets the eye

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: One month following Director Vance kidnapping, Gibbs is assigned the role of acting director in his absence while the team searches worldwide for his whereabouts. Starring Mark Harmon, David McCallum and Rocky Carroll

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: The American crime drama returns with the team still reeling from the Department of Defence investigation, with Ziva, McGee and Tony having handed in their badges. Parsons continues his inquiries into Gibbs and his agents, but events take a turn when an explosion kills the Secretary of the US Navy and seriously injures the director of Homeland Security. When attempts are made on the lives of Gibbs and Tony, it appears that all of the NCIS agents are in mortal danger. Starring Mark Harmon and Colin Hanks

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: Sarah Porter, the new Secretary of the Navy, charges Gibbs and the team with finding terrorist group the Brotherhood of Doubt, which was responsible for killing her predecessor. She expects the group's next target to be billionaire Tomas Mendez, who spends his money trying to defeat terrorism, but whose motives may not be entirely altruistic. Meanwhile, Tony flies to Tel Aviv, where new Mossad director Orli Elbaz reveals they have a report of a possible sighting of Ziva at a kibbutz she went to as a child. Crime drama, guest starring Marina Sirtis

programme.title: Law & Order: Special Victims Unit
programme.desc: A bystander is killed in a robbery, traumatising his mentally ill wife, who is later suspected of physically abusing their child. The police take the youngster into protective custody, but a lack of evidence forces them to return him home - with tragic consequences. Crime drama, starring Christopher Meloni and Mariska Hargitay

programme.title: Law & Order: Special Victims Unit
programme.desc: The body of a teenage girl is found in the boot of a car, and inquiries soon establish she was a bully who enjoyed picking on other girls - leaving Stabler and Benson with a seemingly endless list of suspects to choose from. Hard-hitting drama, starring Christopher Meloni, Mariska Hargitay and Dann Florek

programme.title: Bull
programme.desc: One of Bull's college buddies is accused of murder on a Native American reservation, and the consultant realises the complexity of a tribal court is well beyond his comfort zone

programme.title: Murder, She Wrote

programme.title: Law & Order
programme.desc: The officers leap into action when a financial planner is killed in her apartment building. programme.title: Law & Order
programme.desc: Lupo and Bernard investigate the murder of a man visiting New York City to confront his past, but as they delve into the victim's personal life, the case becomes complicated when all clues point to a revered sheriff. Meanwhile, McCoy faces competition when a new district attorney is nominated for election

programme.title: Law & Order
programme.desc: Lupo and Bernard investigate the murder of memoir writer Sweetie Ness. Clues initially lead them to suspect his stalker, agent and a groupie, but after digging deeper into the victim's past, they begin to consider the possibility he was not who he claimed to be

programme.title: Law & Order
programme.desc: Conclusion of a three-part story. The investigation into a movie studio boss's murder becomes further complicated when fresh evidence points to yet another suspect. Meanwhile, the ex-husband of the deceased goes to trial to face accusations of having a hand in the crime - is an innocent man about to be convicted? Sam Waterston stars

programme.title: Law & Order
programme.desc: Bernard and Lupo investigate the suspected hit-and-run death of the mistress of the CEO of a failed investment bank. The evidence leads the pair to a kidnapping and extortion plot that uncovers several potential suspects, including a disgruntled client

programme.title: Law & Order
programme.desc: The team investigates when a journalist is found murdered, but the case presents new evidence for an old crime involving a man serving a life sentence for spying for the Chinese government. Keith Carradine guest stars

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: Tony is infected with a deadly virus sent through the post by shadowy figures who demand the details of a long-closed case be made public in exchange for the antidote. programme.desc: The team's old enemy Ari Haswari returns, working on behalf of a secretive terrorist faction. Gibbs is distressed to learn he is the next target, but does not realise the killer has another more complex scheme in mind. Guest starring Rudolf Martin, with Mark Harmon

programme.desc: The team is called to investigate a murder committed by members of a religious cult who believe the victim is the son of a woman with psychic powers. Bobby Cannavale guest stars

programme.title: Law & Order: Special Victims Unit
programme.desc: A girl disappears after following her older sister to a college party in Manhattan. programme.title: Law & Order: Special Victims Unit
programme.desc: The team investigates the death of a six-year-old boy who is believed to have collapsed during an exorcism at a late-night church service. programme.desc: The police discover a 12-year-old boy posted sinister threats to a girl in an internet chatroom and suspect his anger stems from sexual abuse. programme.desc: A young teacher faces prosecution for falsifying test results, and she appeals to Jason for legal aid, claiming she only changed the score to help a student reach their potential

programme.desc: A woman's murder leads Lupo and Bernard to learn she was secretly in conflict with her husband, going against his desire that their family lead an environmentally friendly life. Guest starring Ned Beatty (Deliverance) and Sherry Stringfield (ER)

programme.title: Law & Order
programme.desc: Lupo and Bernard investigate when lawyers Fred and Lizzy Bellamy are found murdered in their sleep. The case leads them to two couples who are in the process of adopting children from Haiti, but the detectives uncover signs of abuse that point to a human-trafficking ring

programme.title: Law & Order
programme.desc: A woman is attacked and killed just hours after a serial rapist is freed on parole, prompting McCoy to pull out all the stops to bring him to justice - but before long the detective realises the wanted man could be innocent. Drama, starring Sam Waterston and Jerry Orbach

programme.title: Law & Order
programme.desc: Lupo and Bernard investigate the brutal murder of a TV reporter who had been alleging that a billionaire Wall Street investment adviser was a fraud. Evidence of love triangles and hedge fund fraud soon comes to the fore. Crime drama, starring Jeremy Sisto and Anthony Anderson

programme.title: Law & Order
programme.desc: Lupo and Bernard investigate when an illegal immigrant is attacked, and the incident is connected to a spate of hate crimes against Hispanic men. The prosecution of some teenagers for the crime proves to be a thorny and delicate issue for the District Attorney's office

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: Tony is infected with a deadly virus sent through the post by shadowy figures who demand the details of a long-closed case be made public in exchange for the antidote. McGee and Gibbs set out to identify the culprits and find a cure for the disease. Michael Weatherly and Mark Harmon star

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: The team's old enemy Ari Haswari returns, working on behalf of a secretive terrorist faction. Gibbs is distressed to learn he is the next target, but does not realise the killer has another more complex scheme in mind. Guest starring Rudolf Martin, with Mark Harmon

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: Part one of two. The team reels in the wake of the terrorist attack, but tries to avenge Kate's death by collecting as much information as possible on Ari before he does any more damage. Meanwhile, Gibbs learns one of his old flames has been named as the new NCIS director

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: Part two of two. Gibbs continues to blame himself for Kate's death, and enlists Jenny's help to track down Ari - but they are nearly killed in a shoot-out. Just when all seems lost, however, Ziva is able to point the investigation in the right direction. Starring Mark Harmon and Cote de Pablo

programme.title: The Murders
programme.desc: Kate and Nolan re-open a cold case when they probe the murder of an exonerated man. Investigative drama, starring Jessica Lucas

programme.title: Law & Order: Special Victims Unit
programme.desc: A baby's abduction from a playground leads to a twisted case of lies, betrayal and infidelity. Hard-hitting and emotional series chronicling the life and crimes of the New York Police Department, starring Mariska Hargitay and Dann Florek

programme.title: Law & Order: Special Victims Unit
programme.desc: Reports of sexual abuse surface at an elite private school, prompting Detective Benson and ADA Barba to confront a powerful administrator to expose decades of secrets. Hard-hitting and emotional series chronicling the life and crimes of the New York Police Department, starring Mariska Hargitay and Dann Florek

programme.title: Law & Order: Special Victims Unit
programme.desc: An assistant district attorney is found strangled in his car in an area notorious for prostitution. Benson is shocked to realise she briefly dated the victim and refuses to believe he would have paid for sex. As investigations get under way, DNA evidence reveals the dead man might have been gay. Drama, starring Christopher Meloni and Mariska Hargitay

programme.title: Law & Order: Special Victims Unit
programme.desc: The rape and murder of a criminology student puts detectives Stabler and Benson on the trail of a killer with an expert knowledge of forensics. A discovery about the victim's mentor places him at the top of their list of suspects - but as further evidence is gathered, it begins to seem that someone might have tried to frame him for the crime

programme.title: Law & Order: Special Victims Unit
programme.desc: Detective Munch becomes personally involved in the prosecution of a terminally ill embryologist who is accused of assisting a depressed woman in committing suicide. Evidence points overwhelmingly to her guilt - but the jury finds it hard to decide her fate. Hard-hitting police drama, guest starring Marlee Matlin

programme.title: Bull
programme.desc: Jason finds himself working alone in the run-up to Thanksgiving, as he agrees to help a boxer who is being tried for murder

programme.title: Criminals: Caught on Camera
programme.desc: Journalist Nick Wallis joins police forces around the nation to discover how CCTV and technological advances are helping to bring criminals to justice. The programme reveals how essential surveillance cameras have become in the fight against crime and features footage of people caught red-handed

programme.desc: T'Pol joins the crew on a visit to an ancient Vulcan monastery, despite objecting that her human shipmate's presence could be a disruptive one. On landing, they are shocked to discover the spiritual sanctuary has been taken over by the Andorians, a paranoid race of aliens with a long history of conflict with the Vulcan people. Sci-fi adventure, starring Scott Bakula and Jolene Blalock

programme.title: Merlin
programme.desc: The warlock agrees to help a young druid boy, Daegal, although to do so he has to leave Camelot on a dangerous mission - just as the citadel is playing host to the fearsome Sarrum of Amata. He hopes no one will notice his absence, but as the sky darkens so do Merlin's suspicions - is there more to Daegal than meets the eye? And can a man as ruthless as Sartre be trusted? Fantasy drama, starring Colin Morgan

programme.title: Wynonna Earp
programme.desc: When a series of playing card-related killings affects Wynonna in odd ways, she becomes convinced that the missing revenant is to blame. While she and Haught investigate, Waverly attempts to ditch the family's freakish reputation by hosting a hen party for a friend. Meanwhile, the Doc puts retribution before loyalty. Western drama, starring Melanie Scrofano

programme.title: Zigby
programme.desc: McMeer's home is flooded, so Zigby sets out to build a new one for him

programme.title: Zot the Dog
programme.desc: The tales of Zot, a good-hearted, fun-loving but rather bouncy dog and his best pal, Clive

programme.title: Skippy The Bush Kangaroo
programme.desc: The adventures of a young boy and his pet kangaroo

programme.title: Heidi
programme.desc: Follow Heidi as she discovers life's ups and downs. She lights up and soothes the people and animals all around her, with her enthusiastic, generous and wonderful zest for life

programme.title: Zeek's Animal World
programme.desc: Follow alien robot Zeek as he tries to find out some fascinating facts about our animal kingdom

programme.title: Pocket Dragon Adventures
programme.desc: A group of very small dragons live with a kindly old wizard, they're not big enough to fly but that doesn't stop them having many adventures

programme.title: Walks Around Britain
programme.desc: Andrew White walks along the cliffs at Flamborough Head and through Clumber Park, Nottinghamshire

programme.title: Underworld at War
programme.desc: Peter Williams tells the contrasting stories of people responding to the traumas of the Second World War. While Kent student Daphne Baker set her heart on becoming a head teacher as her contribution to the war effort, conman Neville Heath embarked on a fraudulent spree preying on vulnerable women

programme.title: The Queen's Palaces
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce looks into the past of Edinburgh's Palace of Holyroodhouse, which is located close to the dormant volcano Arthur's Seat. Events in the royal residence, one of the smallest palaces in Britain, have determined the futures of England, France and Scotland, and it was the last place in which a royal prince challenged the right of an English king to sit on the throne. Fiona also examines objects including the Darnley jewel, which features numerous hidden messages, and the needlework of a doomed queen

programme.title: Callan
programme.desc: Spy drama, starring Edward Woodward as an embittered secret agent

programme.title: Callan
programme.desc: Spy drama, starring Edward Woodward as an embittered secret agent

programme.title: The Cat Gang (1959)
programme.desc: A gang of children is always hanging around a harbour - although the customs officers are always trying to get rid of them. When the youngsters stumble on some shady characters behaving suspiciously, they end up uncovering a smuggling plot. Children's crime drama, starring Francesca Annis and John Pike

programme.title: Crimes at the Dark House ()
programme.desc: A criminal murders an aristocrat, planning to assume his identity - but is disappointed to discover the man he has replaced is heavily in debt. He then plots to marry a wealthy heiress for her fortune, but is forced to kill anyone who comes close to learning his true identity. Horror based on Wilkie Collins' The Woman in White, starring Tod Slaughter and Sylvia Marriott

programme.title: The Boy Who Stole a Million (1960)
programme.desc: A boy robs a bank to pay for repairs on his father's car, and is pursued by the police and criminals after the money. Comedy adventure, starring Virgilio Teixeira and Maurice Reyna

programme.title: Danger Man
programme.desc: John Drake (Patrick McGoohan), a secret agent working for NATO, travels to different corners of the world in order to prevent crimes that threaten world peace

programme.title: The Ronnie Wood Show
programme.desc: The Rolling Stones guitarist is joined by fellow musicians for an impromptu jam session, as well as a chat about their lives in rock. In this edition he is joined by Alice Cooper

programme.title: The Ronnie Wood Show
programme.desc: The Rolling Stones guitarist is joined by Stereophonics frontman Kelly Jones, who discusses some of his favourite songs, including Otis Redding's Cigarettes and Coffee and Who Killed Davey Moore? by Bob Dylan

programme.title: After You've Gone
programme.desc: Comedy following the life of divorcee Jimmy Venables after his former wife leaves to perform charity work in Africa, prompting his mother-in-law to move in to the family home. Nicholas Lyndhurst and Celia Imrie star

programme.title: London Districts
programme.desc: Short visual tours exploring and revealing lesser known areas of London for locals and tourists. Narrated by Dewyne Lindsay.

programme.title: A Year at Kew
programme.desc: Alan Titchmarsh, a student at Kew in the 1970s, offers an insight into the work carried out behind the scenes by the 150 staff at the Royal Botanic Gardens, which house a diverse collection of plants and are a leading centre for scientific research and conservation

programme.title: Birds of a Feather
programme.desc: Comedy, starring Pauline Quirke, Linda Robson and Lesley Joseph

programme.title: National Treasure
programme.desc: Paul and his family have to deal with the pressure of mounting allegations against him, and Dee confronts her former babysitter, Christina, now one of the star's accusers. Marie is rattled to discover she was away on the nights Paul is accused of assaulting her, and the accused gives a radio interview, determined to put his side of the story. Drama, starring Robbie Coltrane and Julie Walters

programme.title: Ladies That Punch
programme.desc: Documentary about the Ladies Boxing Association, whose members come from all walks of life, from catwalk models to IT workers

programme.title: Hooligan Escape: The Russian Job (2018)
programme.desc: Five British football fans are arrested while attending the World Cup in Russia. However, their problems get even worse when a corrupt guard hands them over to an old enemy who has been plotting revenge for years. Thriller, starring Ali Bastian and Charlie Wernham

programme.title: One Night in Powder (2015)
programme.desc: A clueless music promoter hits the streets of East London, hoping to put together the greatest club night the capital has ever seen, and recruiting the most eccentric residents of Dalston to help him. Documentary-style comedy, starring Jason Attar

programme.title: True Blue Confessions
programme.desc: A tribute to Mary Millington, Britain's number one sex symbol of the 1970s

programme.title: Ultimate Force
programme.desc: Military drama following life for a group of SAS recruits, starring Ross Kemp

programme.title: Stella Street
programme.desc: The lives, loves and laundry of the famous stars living in leafy Surbiton, as performed by impressionists John Sessions and Phil Cornwell

programme.title: The Food Hospital
programme.desc: The team offers advice to a 19-year-old fitness instructor who claims to have never eaten a vegetable, and a woman whose menopausal hot flushes are ruining her life. Also examined is a 39-year-old photographer whose diet may be causing her spine to fuse. The results of the first Big Food Trial are announced, and Pixie McKenna investigates the reliability of do-it-yourself food-intolerance tests

programme.title: A Fine Romance
programme.desc: Mike is having financial trouble, so Laura decides to help out. Comedy, starring Judi Dench and Michael Williams

programme.title: Pocket Dragon Adventures
programme.desc: A group of very small dragons live with a kindly old wizard, they're not big enough to fly but that doesn't stop them having many adventures

programme.title: Zigby
programme.desc: The lively zebra loses his pineapples and suspects they have been stolen by pirates

programme.title: Zot the Dog
programme.desc: The tales of Zot, a good-hearted, fun-loving but rather bouncy dog and his best pal, Clive

programme.title: Skippy The Bush Kangaroo
programme.desc: The adventures of a young boy and his pet kangaroo

programme.title: Heidi
programme.desc: Follow Heidi as she discovers life's ups and downs. She lights up and soothes the people and animals all around her, with her enthusiastic, generous and wonderful zest for life

programme.title: Zeek's Animal World
programme.desc: Follow alien robot Zeek as he tries to find out some fascinating facts about our animal kingdom

programme.title: Pocket Dragon Adventures
programme.desc: A group of very small dragons live with a kindly old wizard, they're not big enough to fly but that doesn't stop them having many adventures

programme.title: Walks Around Britain
programme.desc: Andrew White treks along the Isle of Man's stunning coast and visits Sprotbrough in South Yorkshire

programme.title: Underworld at War
programme.desc: Peter Williams travels to the Wiltshire village home of Jim Stoodley, who at the age of 14 stole a US aircraft and took to the air with the intention of killing Adolf Hitler - earning himself a place in the Guinness Book of Records as the youngest pilot in history

programme.title: After You've Gone
programme.desc: Comedy following the life of divorcee Jimmy Venables after his former wife leaves to perform charity work in Africa, prompting his mother-in-law to move in to the family home. Nicholas Lyndhurst and Celia Imrie star

programme.title: London Districts
programme.desc: Short visual tours exploring and revealing lesser known areas of London for locals and tourists. Narrated by Dewyne Lindsay.

programme.title: Birds of a Feather
programme.desc: Comedy, starring Pauline Quirke, Linda Robson and Lesley Joseph

programme.title: A Year at Kew
programme.desc: Alan Titchmarsh, a student at Kew in the 1970s, offers an insight into the work carried out behind the scenes by the 150 staff at the Royal Botanic Gardens, which house a diverse collection of plants and are a leading centre for scientific research and conservation

programme.title: Danger UXB
programme.desc: The team deals with a bomb at a gas works, only to discover another explosive device - which is set to go off at a specific time

programme.title: Trottie True (1949)
programme.desc: A strong-willed showgirl marries into the aristocracy after a fling with a pioneering balloonist - but comes to regret her choice. Period comedy, with Jean Kent and James Donald

programme.title: The Upturned Glass (1947)
programme.desc: A surgeon tells a class of students the story of his tragic affair with the married mother of a patient. When she later died under suspicious circumstances, he became convinced she was killed by her jealous sister-in-law, and decided to avenge her death by committing a murder of his own. Drama, starring James Mason, Pamela Kellino and Rosamund John

programme.title: London Districts
programme.desc: Short visual tours narrated by Dewyne Lindsay, this time exploring Camden and Sydenham for locals and tourists.

programme.title: After You've Gone
programme.desc: Comedy following the life of divorcee Jimmy Venables after his former wife leaves to perform charity work in Africa, prompting his mother-in-law to move in to the family home. Nicholas Lyndhurst and Celia Imrie star

programme.title: London Districts
programme.desc: Short visual tours exploring and revealing Crystal Palace and Shoreditch. Narrated by Dewyne Lindsay

programme.title: The Ronnie Wood Show
programme.desc: Producer Mark Ronson compares notes with Ronnie about their varied approaches to the recording process, and the pair also discuss artists including Amy Winehouse and Stevie Wonder

programme.title: The Ronnie Wood Show
programme.desc: The Rolling Stones guitarist and former Faces bandmate Ian McLagan reminisce about their time on the road together in the 1970s, and discuss the band's 2011 reunion tour

programme.title: Danger Man
programme.desc: John Drake (Patrick McGoohan), a secret agent working for NATO, travels to different corners of the world in order to prevent crimes that threaten world peace

programme.title: Rachel Khoo's Kitchen Notebook
programme.desc: The chef explores quirky homespun food trends by visiting a a shop that creates custom preserves, and a home-based salmon smokehouse

programme.title: Birds of a Feather
programme.desc: Comedy, starring Pauline Quirke, Linda Robson and Lesley Joseph

programme.title: Play Me Burlesque ()
programme.desc: Investigating the Coney Island burlesque scene, where burlesque all began. Everyone from dancers to sound technicians has a unique story of how they were drawn to this extravagant art form

programme.title: Desperate Characters (1971)
programme.desc: A burglary turns a middle-aged couple's life together upside down, and makes them question the fundamental values they've always believed in. Drama, starring Shirley MacLaine, Kenneth Mars, Gerald O'Loughlin and Sada Thompson

programme.title: Cam Girl (2018)
programme.desc: Documentary about webcam porn, following the experience of one performer, who has lost custody of her son due to her occupation

programme.title: The Statue (1971)
programme.desc: A linguist's wife sculpts a nude statue of him to celebrate his achievements. However, when she grows weary of his neglect, she reveals that its impressive physique was modelled on another man. Convinced she has taken a lover, he sets out to identify the man behind the sculpture. Comedy, starring David Niven, Virna Lisi and Robert Vaughn

programme.title: The Food Hospital
programme.desc: A 47-year-old man receives a shock when consultant surgeon Shaw Somers reveals he is suffering from metabolic syndrome and his body is more like that of a 60-year-old. A 23-year-old woman seeks help with her extremely bad breath, GP Giovanni Miletto puts together an experimental plan to combat chronic fatigue syndrome, and Pixie McKenna discovers how diet can influence people's chances of developing cancer

programme.title: Walks Around Britain
programme.desc: Andrew White walks along the cliffs at Flamborough Head and through Clumber Park, Nottinghamshire

programme.desc: A new commanding officer pushes the team too hard, leaving the overworked team planning a revolt. Ash and Susan consider their future

programme.title: Manganinnie (1980)
programme.desc: An Australian Aboriginal woman goes in search of her lost tribe, accompanied on her journey by a lost white girl. with whom she forms a close bond despite having no common language. As their journey towards the coast continues, the woman begins to suspect her people and culture may have been all but wiped out by British colonists. Historical drama, starring Mawuyul Yanthalawuy and Anna Ralph

programme.title: Forty Thousand Horsemen (1941)
programme.desc: Three Australian volunteers take part in the last cavalry charge in modern warfare while serving in Palestine. First World War drama, starring Grant Taylor and Betty Bryant

programme.title: London Districts
programme.desc: Short visual tours exploring Bankside and East Dulwich for locals and tourists. Narrated by Dewyne Lindsay

programme.title: Underworld at War
programme.desc: Peter Williams tells the contrasting stories of people responding to the traumas of the Second World War. While Kent student Daphne Baker set her heart on becoming a head teacher as her contribution to the war effort, conman Neville Heath embarked on a fraudulent spree preying on vulnerable women

programme.title: Rachel Khoo's Kitchen Notebook
programme.desc: The chef explores quirky homespun food trends by visiting a a shop that creates custom preserves, and a home-based salmon smokehouse

programme.title: The Ronnie Wood Show
programme.desc: Booker T & the MGs guitarist Steve Cropper, who has worked with artists including Wilson Pickett, Otis Redding and Sam & Dave, talks to Ronnie about his lengthy and colourful career. The pair also discuss the records they both admire

programme.title: The Ronnie Wood Show
programme.desc: The guitarist talks to his current Faces bandmate Mick Hucknall. The duo perform a version of Bob Dylan's One of Us Must Know, and Ronnie shares his memories of Jimi Hendrix

programme.title: All You Need Is Love
programme.desc: The landmark series from the 1970s concludes with a look at the role of spirituality and religion in rock

programme.title: Dispatches
programme.desc: Documentary investigating allegations that a lack of proper controls is allowing financial, physical and even sexual abuse to take place within Britain's mosques. Over a year in the making, this programme exposes how worshippers' generosity can be exploited by religious leaders and how extreme groups can assume control of a community

Romantic comedy, starring Ray Panthaki and Daisy Bevan

programme.title: The Cube
programme.desc: Mollie King from the Saturdays tackles a series of fiendishly difficult challenges as she tries to win up to £250,000 for her chosen charity. Cheered by on bandmates Una Foden, Frankie Sandford, Rochelle Humes and Vanessa White in the studio, the singer is under intense pressure to hold her nerve and avoid slip-ups inside The Cube. Back in 2012, Mo Farah became the first contestant to walk away with the jackpot - a feat nobody else has managed since. How will Mollie compare to the Olympic gold medallist? Phillip Schofield is on hand to provide words of encouragement

programme.title: The Cube
programme.desc: Former EastEnders bad girl Charlie Brooks (Janine) and retired England rugby union player Ben Cohen (Strictly Come Dancing) tackle a series of fiendishly difficult challenges testing skill, dexterity, reaction, wit and wisdom. Cheered by on friends and family in the studio, they're under intense pressure to hold their nerve and avoid slip-ups inside The Cube as they aim for the £250,000 charity jackpot. Each given nine lives at the start, the contestants can also use 'simplify' and 'trial run' lifelines to make the job a little easier. Phillip Schofield is the host

programme.title: The Cube
programme.desc: The game show returns in a midweek slot, with a host of fresh games designed to test contestants' skill, nerve and determination. First up is Derry-Londonderry radiographer Lindsey, who believes that working in the trauma department of Southampton Hospital will help her stay calm under pressure. She is followed by retired Yorkshireman Keith, who at 70 is the oldest person to have appeared on the show. Will his extra years give him an advantage in defeating the Cube? Phillip Schofield hosts

programme.title: Fight Night International
programme.desc: Carl Froch v Andre Ward. Another chance to see the Super Six final at the Boardwalk Hall in Atlantic City, New Jersey, where the WBC and WBA Super Middleweight titles were also on the line. Nottingham's Froch bounced back from his sole career loss on points to Mikkel Kessler to reach this final by winning decisions against Arthur Abraham and Glen Johnson. However, the Cobra faced a difficult task against the American, who was unbeaten in his 24 professional bouts and won light heavyweight gold at the 2004 Olympics
